how_to_request_service_cancellation.answer: Customers can cancel services at any time with no fees or penalties. Escalations team processes cancellations after understanding the reason.
talking_points: No-commitment contract with no cancellation fees or penalties
Management team reviews cancellation reasons to improve services
Escalations team handles the final cancellation process and provides off-boarding details
how_to_request_service_cancellation.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. We have a no-commitment contract, and you can cancel services at any time with no additional fees or penalties. Before we proceed, we'd like to understand your reason for cancellation so we can address any concerns and improve our services. Our escalations team will assist you with the cancellation process.

what_is_rent_processing_service.answer: Rent Processing is a $25/month service that allows tenants to continue paying through Ziprent's portal while owners self-manage other aspects.
talking_points: Costs only $25/month with bank accounts already set up
Tenants can enable auto-payments for convenience
System generates P&Ls and accounting reports automatically
Smooth transition when tenant moves out and property needs re-listing
what_is_rent_processing_service.suggested_response: Before we cancel, we'd like to share our rent processing service for only $25/month. Since the tenants and you already have your bank accounts added, it's convenient to have them continue using the system to pay rent. They can also turn on auto payments, and the system will handle accounting so you can generate P&Ls easily.

how_to_contact_specific_department.answer: Customers should continue working with the specific department (Applications, Leasing, Repairs, etc.) that's already handling their inquiry, using dedicated email addresses and phone numbers.
talking_points: Each department has a dedicated email and phone line in email signatures
Escalations team redirects to appropriate department when needed
Phone calls encouraged for urgent/time-sensitive matters
90+ staff members available daily to assist
how_to_contact_specific_department.suggested_response: Thank you for escalating this to our management team. I see that our [Department] team has been in contact with you regarding your inquiry. Please continue to work with them at [department@ziprent.com], [phone number]. For urgent inquiries, phone calls are encouraged. Please reach out to escalations@ziprent.com to bring any issues to the attention of our management staff.

how_to_request_refund_or_compensation.answer: Escalations team reviews compensation requests case-by-case, often offering management fee refunds, credits, or discounts based on service issues.
talking_points: Refunds typically processed within 2-3 business days once approved
Common amounts: $100-$750 depending on severity
May request review updates in exchange for compensation
Escalations manager has authority to approve refunds
how_to_request_refund_or_compensation.suggested_response: I'm happy to honor this request. I've gone ahead and processed the refund, which should appear in your account within 2-3 business days. I'd appreciate it if you could update your online review to reflect this resolution. Please let me know if you have any questions or if there's anything more I can do to help.

how_to_handle_delayed_email_responses.answer: Ziprent has a policy to respond to every inquiry, but volume may cause delays. Customers should forward missed emails to escalations@ziprent.com.
talking_points: Company-wide policy to respond to every inquiry
High email volume can cause delays beyond 24 hours
Phone calls recommended for time-sensitive issues
Escalations team monitors and ensures follow-up
how_to_handle_delayed_email_responses.suggested_response: I'm sorry to hear that some emails don't get responded to promptly. Please note that we have a company-wide policy to respond to every inquiry. If something is missed, please forward it to escalations@ziprent.com, and I will ensure it gets addressed immediately. For urgent matters, please call us directly.

what_to_do_about_urgent_repair_delays.answer: Urgent repairs are marked as priority and escalations team monitors them. Delays are often due to pending owner approval or tenant/vendor coordination.
talking_points: Repairs team reachable at repairs@ziprent.com, 415-843-5964
Phone line available for urgent inquiries
Most delays involve owner approval of estimates
Escalations team can expedite and monitor progress
what_to_do_about_urgent_repair_delays.suggested_response: Thank you for escalating this urgent repair to our management team. I see that our Repairs team has been working on this and [status update]. I'll personally monitor this to ensure it's resolved immediately. For urgent matters like this, please don't hesitate to call our repairs line at 415-843-5964.

how_to_retrieve_keys_or_lockbox_codes.answer: Lockbox codes are provided via email/SMS. For cancellations, codes are sent in off-boarding email and lockboxes are scheduled for pickup.
talking_points: Digital lockbox codes sent 1 hour before scheduled showings
Codes can be manually provided for move-in day
Physical lockboxes picked up after service cancellation
Owner can request lockbox removal at any time
how_to_retrieve_keys_or_lockbox_codes.suggested_response: I have sent over the lockbox codes to you. We'll work on getting someone out to pick up the lockboxes ASAP. Please let me know if you have any questions or if there's anything I can assist with.

how_to_request_portal_reports_or_documen.answer: Owners can generate reports anytime via the portal under the dropdown menu. Staff can also email specific documents upon request.
talking_points: Log into portal, click email icon in upper right, select 'Reports'
Can generate P&Ls, invoices, and other reports on demand
Staff can email specific documents like receipts or invoices
All documents remain accessible even after service ends
how_to_request_portal_reports_or_documen.suggested_response: Please note that you can generate reports at your convenience via portal. When you log in, click on the right upper icon with your email. From there, under the dropdown, you will see 'Reports.' This gives you the option to generate any report you need. The invoice receipt you requested has been attached for easy access.

how_to_switch_from_management_to_rent_pr.answer: Owners can downgrade from full management to rent processing ($25/month) by contacting escalations team who will update services and notify tenants.
talking_points: Confirm effective date for service switch
Owner must provide direct contact info for tenants
Tenants notified they should contact owner for non-rent issues
Portal continues to process rent payments automatically
how_to_switch_from_management_to_rent_pr.suggested_response: Sounds good, I've moved our services over to rent processing effective [date]. Kindly confirm what contact information you'd like me to share with the tenants so I can update them. They'll continue paying rent through the portal, but will reach out to you directly for other matters.

how_to_handle_tenant_disputes_or_legal_m.answer: Ziprent clarifies they are not legal advisors and cannot make decisions on owner-tenant disputes. They facilitate communication but owners must decide on legal matters.
talking_points: Ziprent is not a legal advisor and cannot provide legal counsel
Owners should consult attorneys for legal guidance
Ziprent can facilitate communication between parties
Property management agreements include indemnity clauses
how_to_handle_tenant_disputes_or_legal_m.suggested_response: We understand this is a challenging situation. However, we must clarify that we are not legal advisors and cannot make decisions on matters like this. We recommend consulting with an attorney for proper legal guidance. We can continue to facilitate communication between you and the tenant, but the final decision rests with you.

how_to_request_online_review_incentive.answer: Ziprent offers $25-$100 gift cards or account credits in exchange for online reviews on Google, Yelp, BBB, Zillow, or Trustpilot.
talking_points: Amounts vary: $25 for tenants, $100 for owners typically
Preferred platforms: Yelp (if active), Google, BBB, Zillow, Trustpilot
Credit issued after review is posted and confirmed
Most business comes from reputation/reviews
how_to_request_online_review_incentive.suggested_response: As most of our business is derived from our reputation, we'd appreciate you taking the time to leave a review for us online. I'm happy to credit you $100 to your bank account to help with this. If you're open to it, I'll send a link to a few sites where we have a profile. Please let me know once the review is posted!

what_if_automated_emails_are_incorrect.answer: Some emails are automated and may send incorrectly. Customers should contact the department directly or escalations to correct automated messaging errors.
talking_points: Automated emails ensure consistent communication but can have errors
Staff reviews and corrects when issues are flagged
Customers should always reach out if info seems wrong
New emails can push threads down in queue
what_if_automated_emails_are_incorrect.suggested_response: I truly apologize if automated messages caused confusion. Some of our emails are automated to ensure timely updates, but I understand this one was incorrect. I've corrected this in our system. For urgent matters, please don't hesitate to call us directly so we can assist immediately.

how_to_handle_homeowners_insurance_requi.answer: Ziprent must be listed as 'Additional Insured' (not just 'Additional Interest') on owner's homeowner's insurance policy per the Property Management Agreement.
talking_points: Required per Property Management Agreement terms
Must be 'Additional Insured' not 'Additional Interest'
Protects both parties from liability issues
Owner must contact insurance agent to update policy
Ziprent cannot manage property without proper coverage
how_to_handle_homeowners_insurance_requi.suggested_response: I received your insurance certificate. Unfortunately, it lists Ziprent as Additional Interest rather than Additional Insured as required. As a property management company, we cannot take the liability risk without being specifically listed as additional insured. Please reach out to your insurance agent to make this update.

how_to_handle_application_verification_i.answer: Applications team assists with technical issues like ID verification errors, income verification, or SSN matching problems. Applicants can upload documents manually or use alternative verification.
talking_points: Contact applications@ziprent.com, 415-843-5973
Can upload SSN card or ID documents manually
Can verify income via Payscore, Truework, or W4
Some verification issues are system/firewall related
Staff can help troubleshoot and provide alternatives
how_to_handle_application_verification_i.suggested_response: We apologize for the verification difficulties. You may send us a copy of your documents to the applications team or upload them directly to your application under the ID section. For income verification, you can also use Payscore or Truework as alternatives. I've CC'd the applications team to assist you further.

how_to_schedule_or_coordinate_vendor_vis.answer: Vendors and tenants coordinate visits directly after owner approves estimates. Ziprent facilitates initial contact but scheduling is between vendor and tenant.
talking_points: Owner approves estimate, then vendor contacts tenant
Tenant and vendor schedule mutually convenient time
Lockbox access can be provided if tenant unavailable
Ziprent monitors completion and processes invoices
Delays often due to tenant or vendor responsiveness
how_to_schedule_or_coordinate_vendor_vis.suggested_response: Per your approval, I'll share the vendor's contact information with the tenant. The tenant and vendor will coordinate directly to schedule a visit at a mutually convenient time. We'll monitor progress and update you once the work is complete.

how_to_handle_security_deposit_deduction.answer: Ziprent provides itemized deductions with receipts per California law. Disputes are reviewed with evidence from move-in/move-out reports. Final decisions rest with owner.
talking_points: California requires itemization for charges over $125
Move-in and move-out walkthroughs compared for damage assessment
Tenant can contest deductions with evidence
Ziprent facilitates but owner makes final decision
Normal wear and tear cannot be charged to tenant
how_to_handle_security_deposit_deduction.suggested_response: We've reviewed the tenant's contestations regarding the security deposit deductions. Based on the move-in and move-out reports, [assessment]. Please review and let us know how you'd like to proceed with each contested item. We'll need your response to move forward with finalizing the deposit.

what_to_do_if_showing_lockbox_missing.answer: If lockbox is missing or moved, customer should call immediately. Staff provides updated location photos or reschedules showing with correct instructions.
talking_points: Call immediately if lockbox not found: 415-877-4515
Staff can provide updated photos of lockbox location
Showing can be rescheduled if unable to access
Lockbox issues are escalated for quick resolution
what_to_do_if_showing_lockbox_missing.suggested_response: We sincerely apologize for this inconvenience. We have sent you updated photos of the lockbox's new location. Please let us know if you need any additional assistance accessing the property. We're here to help.

how_to_dispute_repair_responsibility.answer: Tenant vs. owner responsibility is determined by lease terms, move-in condition, and whether damage is normal wear vs. negligence. Vendor assessment may be needed.
talking_points: Lease specifies tenant vs. owner repair responsibilities
Pre-existing issues documented in move-in report are owner's responsibility
Normal wear and tear is owner's responsibility
Damage from misuse or negligence is tenant's responsibility
Vendor can assess cause of damage
how_to_dispute_repair_responsibility.suggested_response: We can only move forward with charging the tenants if the vendor confirms the damage was caused by misuse or negligence. If it's determined to be normal wear and tear or pre-existing, it would be the owner's responsibility. I recommend approving a vendor to assess and provide that determination.

how_to_handle_late_rent_or_unpaid_fees.answer: Late fees and other charges are added to tenant portal but tenants must initiate payment. If unpaid at move-out, deductions can be made from security deposit.
talking_points: Late fees automatically added to portal after grace period
Tenants must manually initiate payment
Manager team follows up with reminders
Unpaid balances deducted from security deposit at move-out
Collections process available for unpaid amounts
how_to_handle_late_rent_or_unpaid_fees.suggested_response: The late fee and [other charges] have been added to the tenant's portal, but the tenant has not initiated payment yet. We have followed up with them. If they fail to pay before move-out, this will present an outstanding balance that can be deducted from their security deposit.

how_to_get_lease_renewal_processed.answer: Renewals team handles lease renewals, communicates terms with tenants, and coordinates signing. Process starts 60-90 days before lease expiration.
talking_points: Contact renewals@ziprent.com, 415-843-5977
Renewal terms negotiated with owner first
Tenant has time to review and decide
If unsigned, may convert to month-to-month
30-day notice required to vacate
how_to_get_lease_renewal_processed.suggested_response: We're working with the tenants on signing the renewal lease. They are actively reviewing it and we've sent instructions on how to sign. Please note the current lease doesn't expire until [date], so we have time. We'll notify you once the renewal is complete.

what_if_tenant_denies_vendor_access.answer: If tenant denies access, it's documented and owner is notified. Repeated denials may constitute lease violation. Ziprent facilitates rescheduling and communication.
talking_points: 24-48 hour notice typically required for entry
Tenant refusal documented in system
Owner can address lease violations directly
Ziprent helps reschedule and mediate
Urgent health/safety repairs may have different rules
what_if_tenant_denies_vendor_access.suggested_response: I've documented that the tenant denied access to the vendor. We'll work on rescheduling the visit and will reach out to the tenant to arrange a time that works. If this continues to be an issue, we can discuss next steps with you regarding potential lease violations.

how_to_request_discounted_tenant_placeme.answer: Escalations team can offer discounted tenant placement fees ($750-$1,000) for owners considering cancellation or who had service issues.
talking_points: Standard fee reduced to $750-$1,000 in special cases
Offered to retain business or resolve complaints
Covers vetting, lease generation, and placement
Full service vs. partial service pricing varies
how_to_request_discounted_tenant_placeme.suggested_response: We'd really appreciate your business, and we'd be happy to offer a discounted tenant placement fee of $1,000 if you'd still be open to utilizing our services. If you'd still like to proceed with cancellation, we completely understand and will process it upon your response.

how_to_handle_move_in_day_issues.answer: Move-in day issues (missing codes, property not ready) are escalated immediately. Staff provides codes, coordinates with owners, and may arrange temporary solutions.
talking_points: Call immediately for move-in day emergencies
Codes can be provided manually if automated system fails
Property delays may require rent proration or temporary housing discussion
Escalations team monitors until resolved
how_to_handle_move_in_day_issues.suggested_response: I sincerely apologize for this move-in day issue. I'm providing [solution] immediately. For urgent matters like this, please don't hesitate to call us directly at 415-877-4515. I'll personally monitor this to ensure everything is resolved quickly.

how_to_update_tenant_or_owner_contact_in.answer: Contact information is updated in the portal by the user or by staff when notified. Critical for receiving SMS codes, notices, and communications.
talking_points: Users can update their own info in portal settings
Staff can update upon request
Correct phone number needed for SMS lockbox codes
Email changes require verification
Important for receiving time-sensitive notices
how_to_update_tenant_or_owner_contact_in.suggested_response: Thank you for letting us know. I've updated your contact information in our system. Please log into your portal to verify everything is correct. This will ensure you receive all important communications and SMS codes going forward.

what_if_repair_estimate_seems_too_high.answer: Owners can request second opinions, use their own vendors, or have Ziprent collect additional estimates from other vendors for comparison.
talking_points: Owners can request multiple vendor quotes
Owners can use their own preferred vendors
Warranty work should be explored first
Escalations team can help evaluate and collect additional bids
Large estimates require owner approval before proceeding
what_if_repair_estimate_seems_too_high.suggested_response: I understand your concern about the estimate amount. We can reach out to additional vendors for comparison quotes, or you're welcome to use your own vendor. Please let me know how you'd like to proceed, and we'll coordinate accordingly.

how_to_handle_hoa_related_issues.answer: Owners typically handle HOA communications directly. Ziprent facilitates tenant notifications for HOA-scheduled work and can help coordinate access.
talking_points: Owner usually interfaces with HOA directly
Ziprent notifies tenants of HOA inspections/work
Ziprent coordinates tenant access for HOA vendors
HOA violations or fees directed to owner
Some HOA approval needed before repairs
how_to_handle_hoa_related_issues.suggested_response: We'll notify the tenants about the HOA inspection scheduled for [date/time]. We'll ensure they're aware and available for access. Please keep us updated on any additional HOA communications that may affect the tenants or property.

how_to_provide_feedback_or_complaints.answer: Customers can email escalations@ziprent.com or call 415-877-4515 to bring issues to management attention. All feedback is reviewed by department managers.
talking_points: Escalations email: escalations@ziprent.com
Escalations phone: 415-877-4515
All concerns reviewed by management team
Feedback used to improve processes and training
Compensation considered for service failures
how_to_provide_feedback_or_complaints.suggested_response: Thank you for escalating this concern to our management team. Your feedback is important to us and helps us improve our services. I'm reviewing this personally and will ensure it's addressed properly. Please don't hesitate to reach out to escalations@ziprent.com for any future issues needing management attention.

what_to_do_if_property_not_listed_correc.answer: Properties are syndicated to 25+ listing sites. Some sites may delay or have listing issues. Staff can verify listing status and troubleshoot syndication problems.
talking_points: Listings syndicate to 25+ sites including Zillow, Redfin, etc.
Syndication can take a few days
Duplicate sale listings can block rental listings
Staff can verify which sites are active
Does not post to Craigslist/Facebook due to scam concerns
what_to_do_if_property_not_listed_correc.suggested_response: Thank you for bringing this to our attention. I've verified the listing status and [found that/corrected the issue]. Syndications can take a few days to fully update across all platforms. The property is actively listed on [sites]. Please let me know if you have any other concerns.

how_to_handle_utility_billing_disputes.answer: Ziprent processes utility bills paid on owner's behalf and invoices accordingly. Billing disputes require owner to verify with utility company and provide documentation.
talking_points: Ziprent pays utilities then invoices owner (if service included)
Owner receives copy of utility bills
Disputes require owner to contact utility directly
Payment history available in portal
Tenant vs. owner responsibility per lease terms
how_to_handle_utility_billing_disputes.suggested_response: I've reviewed the utility billing for your property. [Clarification of charges]. If you believe there's an error, please contact the utility company directly and provide us with documentation. We'll be happy to review and make any necessary adjustments to your invoice.

can_ziprent_help_with_selling_property.answer: When owners cancel to sell, Ziprent offers to connect them with partners who provide cash offers, but this is optional and not required.
talking_points: Offered when cancellation reason is selling property
Cash offer facilitation available if interested
Not required, just an optional service
No additional fees for this referral
Services cancelled immediately if owner prefers
can_ziprent_help_with_selling_property.suggested_response: We understand you're planning to sell your property. If you're interested in exploring a full cash offer, please let us know and we can help facilitate this. If not, no worries—we've marked the property as cancelled per your request. We wish you luck with the sale!

how_to_handle_owner_unresponsiveness.answer: If owners don't respond to emails/texts/calls for extended periods, escalations team warns that resources cannot continue to be allocated without cooperation.
talking_points: Multiple contact attempts via email, text, and phone
Urgent repairs may be delayed by lack of approval
Continued unresponsiveness may lead to service termination
Insurance and compliance items require timely response
Phone calls recommended for faster resolution
how_to_handle_owner_unresponsiveness.suggested_response: I'm concerned about the lack of response to my team's communications. We aren't able to work effectively when we don't hear back on emails/texts/calls for over a week. We need your prompt response on all pending issues so we can resolve them and move forward. Please reach out as soon as possible.

what_if_online_application_not_working.answer: Technical issues with online applications can be troubleshooted by applications team. Users should verify phone numbers, use proper internet connections, and may upload documents manually.
talking_points: Ensure traditional internet connection (not public WiFi)
Complete phone number verification
Try different browser if issues persist
Can upload documents manually if automated verification fails
Applications team can assist: applications@ziprent.com
what_if_online_application_not_working.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out about the application issue. Please ensure you're using a traditional internet connection and have verified your phone number. If problems persist, you can upload documents manually or contact our applications team at applications@ziprent.com for assistance.

how_long_does_lease_signing_take.answer: Lease is sent for signatures after application approval. Tenants have several days to review and sign. Countersigning by manager happens after both parties sign.
talking_points: Lease sent after application approved by owner
Tenant and owner sign electronically
Manager countersigns after both parties complete
Usually takes 1-3 days total if parties are responsive
Can expedite for specific move-in dates
how_long_does_lease_signing_take.suggested_response: The lease has been sent for signatures. Once you and the tenant have both signed, our team will countersign to finalize it. This typically takes 1-3 days if everyone is responsive. Please let us know if you have a specific deadline we need to meet.

application_status_inquiry.answer: Owners can check application status through their portal or by contacting the leasing team directly.
talking_points: Applications are reviewed as they come in
Leasing team provides updates on applicant progress
Owners are notified when applications are ready for review
application_status_inquiry.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. We are currently processing applications and will update you as soon as screening is complete. You can also check the status in your owner portal or contact our leasing team at leasing@ziprent.com.

applicant_approval_decision.answer: Owners review applicant details including credit, income, and background checks, then approve or decline to proceed.
talking_points: Ziprent provides comprehensive applicant details including ZipScore
Owners make final approval decisions
Can request additional information or negotiate terms before approval
applicant_approval_decision.suggested_response: We've completed processing the applicant and provided their details for your review. Please let us know if you approve moving forward with lease terms, have questions about their qualifications, or would like to decline and continue with other applicants.

lease_signing_status.answer: Tenants receive lease requests via DocuSign and have 24-48 hours to sign before the lease may be archived due to inactivity.
talking_points: Leases sent via DocuSign to all parties
24-hour activity window before archiving
Can extend deadline or resend if needed
lease_signing_status.suggested_response: We've sent the lease request to the tenant. If you haven't seen activity within 24 hours, please let us know if you'd like us to follow up with them or extend the signing deadline.

move_in_date_changes.answer: Move-in dates can be negotiated between owners and tenants, with lease terms updated accordingly.
talking_points: Coordinate with tenant on preferred dates
Update lease documents to reflect changes
Consider rent proration if mid-month
move_in_date_changes.suggested_response: We can reach out to the applicant to see if they're flexible with the move-in date. Once you both agree on timing, we'll update the lease accordingly and proceed with signing.

security_deposit_amount.answer: Security deposits typically equal one month's rent but may be higher (1.5-2x) for applicants with lower credit scores or income concerns.
talking_points: Standard deposit equals monthly rent
Higher deposits for credit below 650 or income concerns
California law limits deposits for qualifying small landlords
security_deposit_amount.suggested_response: The security deposit is set at [amount] based on the applicant's qualifications. This can be adjusted based on credit score, income verification, and your preferences as the owner.

pet_deposit_and_policies.answer: Pet deposits can be requested for pets, but ESA (Emotional Support Animals) cannot be charged deposits or pet rent per fair housing laws.
talking_points: Pet deposits allowed for regular pets
ESA animals exempt from fees per law
Can request updated ESA documentation if needed
pet_deposit_and_policies.suggested_response: The applicant has listed their animal as an ESA with supporting documentation. Under fair housing laws, we cannot charge additional deposits or fees for ESA animals. However, we can request updated documentation if the current letter is outdated.

rent_amount_negotiation.answer: Rent amounts can be negotiated between owners and applicants, with lease terms updated to reflect agreed pricing.
talking_points: Communicate owner's preferred rent amount
Relay tenant counteroffers
Update lease documents once agreed
rent_amount_negotiation.suggested_response: We'll reach out to the applicant to see if they're willing to proceed at your preferred rent of $[amount]. Once we have their response, we can finalize the lease terms or continue negotiations.

credit_and_income_verification.answer: Ziprent conducts soft credit pulls and verifies income through services like Payscore, Ocrolus, or bank statement review.
talking_points: Soft credit inquiries may not show full reports
Income verified through multiple sources
Can request additional documentation if needed
credit_and_income_verification.suggested_response: We've verified the applicant's credit score and income through our screening process. The credit report and income verification documents are available in your portal. Please let us know if you need additional information.

lease_break_early_termination.answer: Tenants requesting lease breaks are responsible for a tenant replacement fee and rent until a new tenant is found, though terms can be negotiated.
talking_points: Tenant replacement fee typically applies
Rent responsibility until replacement found
Military PCS orders have special provisions
lease_break_early_termination.suggested_response: We've received your lease break request. You'll be responsible for the tenant replacement fee and rent payments until a new tenant is secured. We'll work with the owner on timing and coordinate the re-leasing process.

utility_transfer_confirmation.answer: Tenants are responsible for transferring utilities to their name by move-in date and providing proof of transfer.
talking_points: Tenants set up utilities in their name
Confirmation/proof requested by property management
Coordinate any special utility arrangements with owner
utility_transfer_confirmation.suggested_response: Thank you for confirming your utility transfers. Please send us proof of setup for our records, and let us know if you need any utility provider information from us.

showing_and_lockbox_access.answer: Properties use self-guided showings with digital lockbox codes sent via text after scheduling through the Ziprent system.
talking_points: Self-showing system with digital codes
Enable location services and avoid VPN for booking
Codes sent via text after scheduling
showing_and_lockbox_access.suggested_response: Please schedule your showing through our system. Make sure location services are enabled and you're not using a VPN. You'll receive a lockbox code via text shortly before your scheduled time.

renters_insurance_requirement.answer: Tenants must obtain renters insurance naming Ziprent as additional insured before move-in, with ongoing proof required.
talking_points: Renters insurance required per lease
Ziprent added as additional insured
Proof uploaded to compliance portal
renters_insurance_requirement.suggested_response: Please obtain renters insurance and add Ziprent as Additional Interest at 1550G Tiburon Blvd 343, Tiburon, CA 94920. Upload your declarations page to your portal or email to compliance@ziprent.com.

forwarding_notices_to_tenants.answer: Owners can request that HOA notices, painting schedules, and other property communications be forwarded to tenants.
talking_points: Property management forwards notices as requested
Important to keep tenants informed of property events
Confirmation sent when notices delivered
forwarding_notices_to_tenants.suggested_response: Thank you for sharing this notice. We have forwarded it to your tenants and will ensure they're aware of the upcoming [painting/maintenance/etc.].

bank_account_setup.answer: Owners must link their bank account in the portal for rent payments before tenants can complete lease signing.
talking_points: Bank account required for rent distribution
Links through owner portal
Blocks lease completion until set up
bank_account_setup.suggested_response: We're ready to proceed with the lease, but we need you to link your bank account in the owner portal first. Once completed, we can finalize the lease signing process with your tenant.

section_8_voucher_applicants.answer: Owners cannot legally decline applicants solely because they have Section 8 vouchers, as it's protected under fair housing laws.
talking_points: Section 8 rejection is illegal discrimination
Section 8 provides guaranteed government payment
Lower default rates than traditional tenants
section_8_voucher_applicants.suggested_response: We cannot legally reject applicants because they are Section 8 voucher holders. Section 8 provides guaranteed payment from the government and statistically lower default rates. We recommend proceeding with standard screening criteria.

how_to_request_application_fee_refund.answer: Application fees can be refunded if the property is leased, canceled, or as a courtesy. Refunds typically take 3-5 business days to reflect in the applicant's account.
talking_points: Refunds are processed for duplicate applications, property cancellations, or when property is leased to another applicant
Refund timeline is 3-5 business days depending on the bank
As a courtesy, applications can be transferred to another property instead of refunded
how_to_request_application_fee_refund.suggested_response: We have processed your application fee refund. Please allow 3-5 business days for it to reflect in your account, depending on your bank. If you'd like to apply for another property, we can also transfer your application instead. Let us know how you'd prefer to proceed.

how_to_withdraw_or_cancel_application.answer: Applicants can withdraw by notifying Ziprent via email. If the application was paid, a refund is typically provided or the application can be transferred to another property.
talking_points: Simply reply to confirm you want to withdraw
Paid applications can be refunded or moved to another listing
Unpaid applications are removed from the system immediately
how_to_withdraw_or_cancel_application.suggested_response: Thank you for letting us know. We've removed your application from the property as requested. If you'd like to apply for another property in the future, feel free to browse our available listings at ziprent.com/available-listings. Wishing you all the best in your housing search!

how_to_update_move_in_date.answer: Applicants can update their move-in date by logging into their portal or by emailing the updated date, which will be relayed to the owner.
talking_points: Changes can be made through the application portal or by email
Updated dates are communicated to the property owner
Flexibility with move-in dates can strengthen applications
how_to_update_move_in_date.suggested_response: Thank you for the update! We have noted that your preferred move-in date is now [DATE]. We will relay this to the owner and keep you posted on any further developments.

what_if_income_verification_fails_or_sho.answer: If Payscore/Truework shows incorrect income, applicants can upload paystubs, offer letters, or bank statements for manual verification. Common issues include recent job changes or multiple income sources.
talking_points: Manual verification is available using paystubs, offer letters, or bank statements
System may not capture recent job changes or multiple income sources
Recent paystubs should be within 45 days and show full details
Self-employed applicants can provide tax returns or 1099s
what_if_income_verification_fails_or_sho.suggested_response: Thank you for letting us know. We can verify your income manually using recent paystubs, your offer letter, or bank statements that show deposits. Please upload these documents to your application, or reply to this email with them attached, and we'll update your income accordingly.

how_to_complete_income_verification.answer: Income can be verified through Payscore (bank connection) or Truework (employment verification), or by uploading paystubs, offer letters, W-2s, or tax returns.
talking_points: Log into application portal at ziprent.com/auth/login
Click the income verification link in the application
Alternative: upload recent paystubs (within 45 days) or offer letters
Self-employed can provide tax returns, 1099s, or bank statements
how_to_complete_income_verification.suggested_response: To complete your income verification, please log in at ziprent.com/auth/login and click the verification link in your application. This will take just a few moments through Payscore or Truework. Alternatively, you can upload your most recent paystubs or offer letter. Let us know if you need any assistance!

how_to_upload_or_reupload_id.answer: IDs must be valid, unexpired, government-issued, and in JPG/PNG or PDF format. Upload through the application portal under identity verification section.
talking_points: Accepted IDs: driver's license, passport, state ID
Must be current/unexpired and clearly legible
Upload in JPG, PNG, or PDF format (not screenshots)
Access portal at ziprent.com/auth/login to resubmit
how_to_upload_or_reupload_id.suggested_response: We noticed that your ID could not be read by our system. Please re-upload a clear photo of your valid, government-issued ID in JPG, PNG, or PDF format. You can do this by logging in at ziprent.com/auth/login, going to Applications, and selecting View/Edit. Let us know if you need help!

how_to_fix_credit_frozen_or_no_hit_error.answer: If credit shows as frozen or 'no hit', applicants must unfreeze credit with TransUnion or upload a credit report from Credit Karma or another source showing name, score, and date.
talking_points: Unfreeze credit with TransUnion before rerunning the check
Alternative: upload credit report from creditkarma.com
Report must show applicant's name, score, date, and credit bureau
SSN mismatch can also cause 'no hit' - verify SSN is entered correctly
how_to_fix_credit_frozen_or_no_hit_error.suggested_response: We're unable to pull your credit because it appears frozen. Please unfreeze your credit with TransUnion and let us know when it's done so we can run it again. Alternatively, you can upload a credit report from creditkarma.com that shows your name, score, and date. Let us know if you need guidance!

what_if_ssn_does_not_match_or_no_ssn.answer: SSN mismatches can occur from name spelling errors or incorrect entry. Applicants without SSN (international applicants) can enter nine zeros and provide passport or visa instead.
talking_points: Check that SSN matches legal name exactly as it appears on Social Security card
Verify first/middle/last name spelling
International applicants without SSN can use nine zeros
Use passport or visa for ID verification if no SSN
what_if_ssn_does_not_match_or_no_ssn.suggested_response: We're seeing an SSN mismatch, which usually happens if the name is spelled differently than on your Social Security card. Please double-check your SSN and verify your full legal name matches exactly. If you don't have an SSN, you can enter nine zeros and upload your passport or visa as ID.

how_to_check_application_status.answer: Applications are forwarded to owners once complete. Response time varies by owner. Applicants are updated via email once the owner makes a decision.
talking_points: Completed applications are sent to owners for review within 24 hours
Owner response times vary - some respond quickly, others take several days
Ziprent follows up with owners but cannot control their timeline
Applicants receive email updates when there's news
how_to_check_application_status.suggested_response: Thank you for checking in. We have forwarded your completed application to the property owner for review and are awaiting their response. Response times vary by owner, but we've already followed up with them and will update you as soon as we hear back. Feel free to reach out anytime for status updates.

can_i_offer_higher_deposit_or_prepaid_re.answer: Yes, applicants can offer 1.5-2x security deposit or prepay first/last month's rent to strengthen their application. This is communicated to the owner but approval is at their discretion.
talking_points: Common options: 1.5-2x security deposit, first and last month upfront, or 3-6 months prepaid rent
These offers are relayed to the owner but don't guarantee approval
Higher deposits are refundable at move-out (minus any damages)
Can help compensate for lower credit or income
can_i_offer_higher_deposit_or_prepaid_re.suggested_response: Yes, you can certainly offer a higher security deposit or prepay rent to strengthen your application. Common options include paying 1.5-2x the security deposit or the first and last month's rent upfront. We'll relay your offer to the owner and keep you updated on their decision.

how_to_add_or_complete_co_applicant_appl.answer: All adults must submit separate applications with their own email addresses. Each application requires ID, income verification, and $40 fee. Applications are automatically grouped if they list each other.
talking_points: Each adult needs a unique email and separate application
All applicants pay $40 per person for background checks
List other occupants in 'Additional Occupants' section to group applications
Share application link: app.ziprent.com/rental-application/[PROPERTY_ID]
how_to_add_or_complete_co_applicant_appl.suggested_response: All adults occupying the property need to submit their own complete application. Each person should use a unique email address and complete the application at [LINK]. Make sure to list each other in the Additional Occupants section so your applications are grouped together. Each application requires a $40 fee for background screening.

what_is_a_guarantor_and_how_do_they_appl.answer: A guarantor/co-signer does not occupy the property but provides financial backing. They complete a separate application checking the 'Guarantor' box and listing occupants in the Additional Occupants section.
talking_points: Guarantors check the 'Financial Guarantor/Co-signer' box in application
They do not live at the property but guarantee rent payment
Must list all actual occupants in Additional Occupants section
Guarantors need strong credit/income to compensate for occupants
what_is_a_guarantor_and_how_do_they_appl.suggested_response: A guarantor is someone who financially backs your application but won't live at the property. They should complete their own application, check the 'Financial Guarantor/Co-signer' box, and list all occupants in the Additional Occupants section. The guarantor's income and credit should be strong enough to support the application.

how_to_apply_with_section_8_voucher.answer: Section 8 applicants should upload their voucher and proof of income for their portion of rent. The voucher amount and personal income should be documented. Applications cannot be denied based on credit score if Section 8.
talking_points: Upload current Section 8 voucher showing approved amount
Provide proof of income for tenant's portion of rent
Include caseworker contact information
Credit requirements may be waived but income verification still required
how_to_apply_with_section_8_voucher.suggested_response: Thank you for applying! Please include your Section 8 voucher and proof of income for your portion of the rent with your application. Make sure to upload documentation showing the voucher amount and your caseworker's contact information. We'll review everything and keep you updated.

how_to_apply_without_us_credit_history.answer: International applicants or those without US credit can enter nine zeros for SSN, upload passport/visa as ID, provide international income documents, and consider adding a guarantor with US credit history.
talking_points: Use nine zeros if no SSN available
Upload passport or visa for identification
Provide offer letter, international paystubs, or bank statements
Adding a US-based guarantor significantly strengthens application
Explain situation to owner via application notes
how_to_apply_without_us_credit_history.suggested_response: Since you don't have US credit history, please enter nine zeros for SSN and upload your passport or visa as ID. For income, provide your offer letter and any available paystubs or bank statements. Adding a guarantor with established US credit would significantly strengthen your application. We'll make sure the owner understands your situation.

what_if_new_job_hasnt_started_yet.answer: Applicants with new jobs can upload their signed offer letter showing salary and start date. Manual income verification will be performed based on the contract terms.
talking_points: Upload fully executed offer letter with salary and start date
Must be official contract with signature, not just offer
May be asked to provide first paystub once employment begins
Explain situation in application notes or via email
what_if_new_job_hasnt_started_yet.suggested_response: Thank you for letting us know. Please upload your signed offer letter showing your position, salary, and start date. As long as it's a complete employment contract, we can verify your income manually based on that. We'll review it and move your application forward accordingly.

how_to_fix_duplicate_application_or_paym.answer: Duplicate applications are removed and fees are automatically refunded within 3-5 business days. The correct application is kept active.
talking_points: System sometimes creates duplicates due to technical glitches
Ziprent identifies and removes duplicates
Refunds processed automatically within 3-5 business days
Correct application remains active and moves forward
how_to_fix_duplicate_application_or_paym.suggested_response: We've identified and removed your duplicate application. The duplicate application fee has been refunded and should reflect in your account within 3-5 business days. Your correct application remains active and is being processed normally.

how_to_reaccess_incomplete_application.answer: Log in at ziprent.com/auth/login using the email from the application. Use 'Forgot Password' if needed, then go to Applications tab and select View/Edit to complete missing items.
talking_points: Go to ziprent.com/auth/login
Use email address from original application
Click 'Forgot Password' to reset if needed
Navigate to Applications tab and click View/Edit
Complete missing sections and click Update Application to save
how_to_reaccess_incomplete_application.suggested_response: You can access your application by logging in at ziprent.com/auth/login using the email you applied with. If you don't remember your password, click 'Forgot Password' to reset it. Once logged in, go to the Applications tab, select your application, and click View/Edit to complete any missing information.

what_are_qualification_criteria.answer: Standard criteria are combined group income of 3x monthly rent and credit score of 650+ for all applicants. Owners may have additional requirements or flexibility.
talking_points: Income: Total group income must be 3x the monthly rent
Credit: Each applicant should have 650+ credit score
All adults must complete applications and background checks
Owners have final say and may consider compensating factors
what_are_qualification_criteria.suggested_response: The standard qualification criteria are a combined group income of at least 3x the monthly rent and a credit score of 650+ for all applicants. However, the owner makes the final decision and may consider other factors like employment history, rental references, or offers for higher deposits.

can_i_speak_directly_with_owner.answer: Ziprent manages applications on the owner's behalf. Direct owner contact is not typical, but owner interviews may be arranged if requested and approved.
talking_points: Ziprent handles all application communications as property manager
Owner contact information is not shared for privacy/security
Any questions or offers can be communicated through Ziprent
Owner interviews may be scheduled in some cases after application review
can_i_speak_directly_with_owner.suggested_response: We manage the property on the owner's behalf, so all communication goes through us to ensure a smooth process. If there's anything specific you'd like to discuss or relay to the owner, please let us know and we'll make sure they receive the information. In some cases, the owner may request a call after reviewing applications.

what_does_pending_lease_or_backup_applic.answer: When a lease offer is pending, other applications are kept as backups in case the primary applicant doesn't complete the lease. Applications continue to be accepted and processed.
talking_points: Primary applicant has lease offer but hasn't paid deposit yet
Deals often fall through at the last minute
Backup applications are fully processed and ready
Property is not final until deposit is paid and lease signed
Continue processing applications until lease is fully executed
what_does_pending_lease_or_backup_applic.suggested_response: The owner has extended a lease offer to another applicant, but it's pending their signatures and deposit payment. This often falls through, so we're continuing to process applications including yours as backups. We'll keep you updated on any changes, and you remain a strong candidate should the current offer not go through.

how_to_transfer_application_to_different.answer: Applications can be transferred to another available Ziprent property as a one-time courtesy. Applicants browse listings at ziprent.com/available-listings and notify Ziprent of their choice.
talking_points: Transfer is offered when original property is leased or cancelled
Browse available properties at ziprent.com/available-listings
One-time courtesy per application set
All application information and fees transfer to new property
how_to_transfer_application_to_different.suggested_response: We'd be happy to transfer your application to another property. Please browse our available listings at ziprent.com/available-listings and let us know which property interests you. We'll move your application over as a one-time courtesy, so you won't need to reapply or pay new fees.

what_if_pet_or_esa_documentation_needed.answer: If listing a pet as ESA, applicants must upload ESA letter from licensed healthcare provider on official letterhead with license number, type, jurisdiction, and current date (within last year).
talking_points: ESA letters must be current (within one year)
Must be on official letterhead from licensed provider
Include provider's license number, type, and jurisdiction
If no ESA, change pet selection to regular pet or 'No'
Regular pets subject to owner's pet policy
what_if_pet_or_esa_documentation_needed.suggested_response: Since you indicated your pet is an ESA, please upload your ESA letter. It must be written on official letterhead by a licensed physician or mental health professional, include their license number and type, and be dated within the last year. If you don't have ESA documentation, please update your application to change the pet designation.

how_to_verify_self_employment_income.answer: Self-employed applicants can verify income through tax returns (1099, Schedule C), bank statements showing deposits, profit/loss statements, or business contracts. Payscore can connect to business bank accounts.
talking_points: Upload last 1-2 years of tax returns showing business income
Bank statements showing consistent deposits work well
Payscore can connect to business bank accounts
Profit/loss statements and contracts accepted
CPA letter on letterhead can supplement documentation
how_to_verify_self_employment_income.suggested_response: For self-employment income, please upload your most recent tax returns (1099s or Schedule C) or connect your business bank account through Payscore. Bank statements showing consistent deposits or a profit/loss statement also work. Let us know if you need help determining which documents would work best.

what_if_application_denied_or_owner_choo.answer: When owners select another applicant, remaining applicants are notified. Refunds or application transfers to other properties are typically offered.
talking_points: Owner makes final selection based on qualifications and fit
Ziprent doesn't control owner decisions
Applicants can receive refund or transfer to another listing
Multiple qualified applicants compete for same property
First come doesn't guarantee selection
what_if_application_denied_or_owner_choo.suggested_response: We regret to inform you that the owner has decided to proceed with another applicant. We understand this isn't the news you were hoping for, but the final decision rests with the owner. We can offer you a refund or transfer your application to another property. Please let us know how you'd like to proceed.

what_if_paystubs_are_too_old.answer: Paystubs must be within 45 days. Older paystubs should be replaced with more recent ones or use Payscore for bank-based verification covering the last 12 months.
talking_points: Paystubs must show work performed within last 45 days
Upload 1-2 most recent paystubs
Alternative: use Payscore which looks at 12 months of deposits
Paystubs must show full details: name, employer, pay period, gross/net
what_if_paystubs_are_too_old.suggested_response: The paystubs you uploaded are older than 45 days. Please upload your 1-2 most recent paystubs showing work performed within the last 45 days. Alternatively, you can verify income through Payscore, which evaluates 12 months of bank deposits and may show a more accurate picture of your income.

what_if_income_is_zero_or_unemployed.answer: Applicants with no income should note this and add a guarantor, or provide proof of savings/assets. Zero income must be confirmed deliberately, not left blank.
talking_points: Zero income applicants should have guarantor with qualifying income
Can provide bank statements showing sufficient savings
Explain situation: student, supported by spouse/family, between jobs
Mark income as $0 intentionally to complete application
what_if_income_is_zero_or_unemployed.suggested_response: We see your income is listed as $0. If this is accurate, please confirm so we can complete your application. We recommend adding a guarantor with qualifying income to strengthen your application, or you can provide bank statements showing sufficient savings to cover rent. Please let us know how you'd like to proceed.

how_long_does_application_review_take.answer: Application review timeline varies by owner, typically 24-72 hours, but can take longer. Ziprent sends applications once complete and follows up with owners regularly.
talking_points: Completed applications forwarded to owners within 24 hours
Owner review time varies - some respond same day, others take a week
Ziprent follows up with owners but cannot control their timeline
High demand properties may have faster turnaround
Applicants notified immediately when owner makes decision
how_long_does_application_review_take.suggested_response: Once your application is complete, it's sent to the owner for review within 24 hours. Review time varies by owner - some respond very quickly while others take several days. We've already followed up with the owner and will notify you immediately once we receive their decision. Thank you for your patience!

what_if_credit_score_below_650.answer: Applicants with credit scores below 650 may still apply; owner may request higher security deposit or require a co-signer.
talking_points: Can still apply with lower credit
Owner may request higher deposit
Co-signer option available
Owner makes final decision
what_if_credit_score_below_650.suggested_response: You can still apply even if you don't meet the credit score requirement. The owner may ask for a higher security deposit or you can include a co-signer with your application. Please contact us at 415-688-6660 to discuss your options.

what_if_application_stuck_or_wont_submit.answer: Application submission errors usually indicate missing required fields marked with asterisks, incorrect formats, or technical issues. Check all required fields and contact support if issues persist.
talking_points: All fields marked with asterisk (*) must be completed
Check previous address section is filled
Pet section must be completed even if selecting 'No'
Try different browser or clear cache
Contact applications team if technical issue persists
what_if_application_stuck_or_wont_submit.suggested_response: If your application won't submit, please check that all required fields marked with an asterisk (*) are completed, including previous address and pet information. Make sure you're using a supported browser. If the issue continues, please reply to this email or call us at (415) 843-5973 and we'll help troubleshoot.

what_if_language_barrier_or_need_spanish.answer: Ziprent offers Spanish-speaking support. International applicants can also have family members help with applications or request translated assistance.
talking_points: Spanish-speaking staff available upon request
Family members can help complete applications
All official documents still need to be in English or translated
Call or email to request language assistance
what_if_language_barrier_or_need_spanish.suggested_response: ¡Hola! Tenemos personal que habla español disponible para ayudarte. Por favor, llámanos al (415) 843-5973 o responde a este correo indicando que necesitas asistencia en español. Estaremos encantados de ayudarte con tu solicitud.

when_should_i_expect_to_hear_about_lease.answer: Lease offers are sent after owner completes review of all applications. Timeline varies but Ziprent notifies applicants immediately when offers are made or when property is leased.
talking_points: Owner reviews all completed applications before making decision
Some owners decide quickly, others take several days to compare options
Ziprent follows up regularly but cannot rush owner decisions
Immediate notification sent when lease offer extended or property leased
when_should_i_expect_to_hear_about_lease.suggested_response: The owner is currently reviewing your application along with others received. We've followed up with them and will notify you immediately once they make a decision. Response times vary by owner, but rest assured you'll hear from us as soon as there's any update.

can_application_fee_be_paid_differently.answer: Application fees must be paid through the Ziprent portal using credit/debit card. Wire transfers, cash, or checks are not accepted for application fees.
talking_points: Fees paid online through secure portal only
Credit and debit cards accepted
Wire transfer not available for application fees (only for deposits)
$40 per adult applicant
can_application_fee_be_paid_differently.suggested_response: Application fees must be paid through our secure online portal using a credit or debit card. Unfortunately, we cannot accept wire transfers, cash, or checks for application fees. The fee is $40 per adult applicant and is used to cover background and credit screening costs.

what_if_property_cancelled_or_delisted.answer: When owners cancel or delist properties, applicants are notified and offered refunds or transfers to other available properties.
talking_points: Owner may cancel due to personal circumstances or market changes
Ziprent has no control over owner cancellations
Full refund offered or application transferred to different property
Refund timeline: 3-5 business days
what_if_property_cancelled_or_delisted.suggested_response: We regret to inform you that the property has been cancelled and removed from our listings due to the owner's decision. We sincerely appreciate the time you invested in your application. We can offer you a full refund or transfer your application to another property. Please let us know how you'd like to proceed.

lease_renewal_terms.answer: Owners need to specify renewal offer terms (rent amount, lease length) which Ziprent will present to tenants along with their renewal interest notification.
talking_points: Owner and tenant receive renewal notices simultaneously to gauge interest
Owner must provide specific renewal terms (rent, duration) for Ziprent to present
Ziprent facilitates the renewal offer process between both parties
lease_renewal_terms.suggested_response: We need to know what renewal terms you'd like to offer the tenants (rent amount and lease duration). A notice has been sent to the tenants simultaneously to gauge their interest in renewing. Once you provide your terms, we'll present the formal offer to them.

security_deposit_payment_timing.answer: Security deposit is due at lease signing, while first month's rent is due by the lease start date. Payments can be spread between these milestones.
talking_points: Security deposit required at time of lease signing to secure the property
First month's rent payment deadline is the lease start date
This timeline helps tenants spread upfront costs over several weeks
security_deposit_payment_timing.suggested_response: The security deposit is required at the time of lease signing to secure your lease. You have until the lease start date to pay the first month's rent, which helps spread out the upfront costs.

walkthrough_scheduling.answer: Tenants should complete property walkthrough within 10 days before move-in date using the smart lockbox and submit the walkthrough form online.
talking_points: Walkthrough must be scheduled at least 10 days prior to move-in
Self-guided tour using smart lockbox code provided by Ziprent
Required walkthrough form must be submitted online after inspection
walkthrough_scheduling.suggested_response: Please schedule your walkthrough within 10 days before your move-in date. You'll receive a smart lockbox code to access the property. After your walkthrough, complete and submit the required walkthrough form at the link we'll provide.

lockbox_code_access.answer: Lockbox codes are provided after security deposit and first month's rent are paid, and become active on the move-in date for key retrieval.
talking_points: Code issued once all move-in payments (deposit and rent) are completed
Code becomes active on scheduled move-in date
Lockbox remains onsite during lease term for property access needs
lockbox_code_access.suggested_response: Your lockbox code will be provided once both the security deposit and first month's rent have been received. The code will become active on your move-in date, allowing you to retrieve the keys from the lockbox at the property.

renters_insurance_requirements.answer: All tenants must provide proof of renter's insurance before move-in, with all renters listed on the policy. Failure to provide may result in occupancy refusal.
talking_points: Renter's insurance is mandatory per lease agreement
All tenants on lease must be included on the insurance policy
Proof must be uploaded to portal before move-in date or occupancy may be refused
renters_insurance_requirements.suggested_response: All tenants on the lease must have renter's insurance with everyone listed on the policy. Please upload proof of insurance to your portal before move-in. Failure to provide this documentation may result in refusal of occupancy per your lease terms.

utility_setup_questions.answer: Tenants are responsible for setting up utilities in their name by the move-in date. Ziprent provides utility provider information and previous account numbers when available.
talking_points: Tenants must transfer utilities into their name effective move-in date
Ziprent provides utility company names and account numbers from owner
Service should be scheduled to start on or before move-in date
utility_setup_questions.suggested_response: You're responsible for setting up utilities in your name by your move-in date. We'll provide the utility company information and previous account numbers when available from the owner. Please schedule service to begin on your move-in date.

lease_term_clarifications.answer: Lease terms including rent amount, deposit, fees, and dates are confirmed before signing. Any discrepancies should be reported immediately for correction.
talking_points: Review all lease terms carefully including rent, deposit, fees, and dates
Report any discrepancies immediately to leasing team
Lease will be corrected and reissued for all parties to re-sign if needed
lease_term_clarifications.suggested_response: Please review all lease terms carefully including the rent amount, security deposit, additional fees, and lease dates. If you notice any discrepancies from what was discussed, contact us immediately so we can correct the lease and have all parties re-sign.

early_access_requests.answer: Early access before lease start date requires owner approval. Requests for early move-in, measurements, or furniture delivery must be coordinated through Ziprent.
talking_points: All early access requests require owner's explicit approval
Common requests include taking measurements, furniture delivery, or early move-in
Ziprent coordinates timing and conditions with owner before granting access
early_access_requests.suggested_response: We'll check with the owner regarding your request for early access to the property. All early access before your lease start date requires owner approval. We'll update you shortly once we receive their response.

deposit_amount_calculations.answer: Security deposits are typically 1.5x-2x monthly rent depending on credit scores and property type, with California legal maximums at 2x rent (unfurnished) or 3x rent (furnished).
talking_points: Standard deposits range from 1.5x to 2x monthly rent based on qualifications
California law caps deposits at 2x rent for unfurnished, 3x for furnished properties
Additional pet deposits may apply within legal limits
deposit_amount_calculations.suggested_response: The security deposit is calculated based on your qualifications and is set at [amount], which is [multiplier]x the monthly rent. This falls within California's legal maximum of 2x rent for unfurnished properties. Additional pet deposits may apply if pets are approved.

lease_signing_pending_items.answer: Before finalizing the lease, tenants must accept terms, verify ID, pay deposit, and complete background checks. Owners must also approve and sign.
talking_points: Tenants must accept lease terms, verify photo ID, and initiate security deposit
Background check results must be reviewed and cleared
Owner must review, approve, and sign before Ziprent countersigns to finalize
lease_signing_pending_items.suggested_response: Before the lease is finalized, we need all tenants to accept the terms, verify their photo IDs, and initiate the security deposit payment. We'll also complete background check reviews. Once everything is confirmed and the owner signs, we'll countersign to finalize your lease.

key_and_access_handover.answer: Keys are provided via lockbox on move-in date after all payments clear. Additional keys, garage remotes, and access codes are left inside the property or specified locations.
talking_points: Main keys available in lockbox with code provided after payment clears
Additional keys, remotes, and access devices typically left inside property
Smart lock codes and gate access information provided in move-in guide
key_and_access_handover.suggested_response: Your main keys will be in the lockbox, accessible with the code we provide after your payments clear. Additional keys, garage remotes, and other access devices will be left inside the property as specified in your move-in guide. Smart lock codes and gate access information will also be included.

payment_processing_issues.answer: Payment issues are typically due to insufficient funds, bank restrictions, or incorrect account linking. Ziprent works with tenants to resolve through alternative payment methods or bank verification.
talking_points: Common issues include insufficient funds or bank-side transaction blocks
Tenants should verify account balance and contact their bank if payment fails
Ziprent can provide alternative payment methods or troubleshoot portal issues
payment_processing_issues.suggested_response: We're seeing that your payment was declined by your bank. Please verify you have sufficient funds and that your bank hasn't blocked the transaction. Contact your bank to authorize the payment, or reach out to us at 415-843-5971 to discuss alternative payment methods.

owner_questions_before_signing.answer: Tenants wanting to contact owners directly with questions before signing must request permission through Ziprent, who typically relays questions on tenant's behalf.
talking_points: Ziprent serves as intermediary for tenant-owner communications
Questions can be relayed to owner through leasing team for faster response
Direct contact only arranged with owner's explicit permission
owner_questions_before_signing.suggested_response: I'd be happy to relay your questions to the owner on your behalf for a quick response. If you prefer to speak with the owner directly before placing your deposit, I can check with them to confirm their availability and willingness to connect directly.

property_condition_concerns.answer: Pre-move-in repairs and maintenance issues should be reported immediately. Ziprent coordinates with owners to address items before or shortly after move-in.
talking_points: Report any property condition concerns from walkthrough immediately
Ziprent coordinates repair timeline with owner before move-in when possible
Non-urgent items may be scheduled for after move-in with tenant's consent
property_condition_concerns.suggested_response: Thank you for reporting these property condition issues. We're coordinating with the owner to address these items before your move-in date. We'll keep you updated on the repair timeline and confirm when the work has been completed.

move_in_guide_information.answer: Move-in guides are sent after lease finalization and include property-specific information about mailbox location, parking, utilities, HOA rules, and access codes.
talking_points: Comprehensive move-in guide sent after lease is fully executed
Includes mailbox location, parking assignments, utility providers, and HOA information
Contains smart lock codes, gate access, and property-specific instructions
move_in_guide_information.suggested_response: You'll receive a comprehensive move-in guide shortly after the lease is finalized. It will include property-specific information such as mailbox location, parking details, utility provider contacts, HOA rules, and all access codes you'll need for move-in.

how_to_relay_information_to_tenant.answer: Ziprent relays owner messages, HOA notices, and updates to tenants as a standard service. Owners can request information be communicated to tenants.
talking_points: Manager team relays information to tenants on behalf of owners
Works for HOA notices, maintenance schedules, landscaper visits, policy updates
Confirmation provided once tenant has been notified
Owners should forward notices or provide details they want shared
how_to_relay_information_to_tenant.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. We will relay this information to the tenant(s) and update you once they've been notified. Please let us know if you need anything else.

how_to_notify_tenant_about_late_rent.answer: When tenants notify of late rent, the manager team informs owners and notes that late fees will automatically apply after the grace period expires.
talking_points: Manager team relays tenant's late payment notification to owner
Late fees are automatically applied after grace period
Three-day pay or quit notice processed after grace period
System automatically handles fee assessment
how_to_notify_tenant_about_late_rent.suggested_response: Thank you for letting us know. We will inform the owner accordingly. Please note that a late fee will be automatically applied, and a three-day pay or quit notice will be processed after the grace period has passed.

how_to_request_rent_credit.answer: Rent credits are calculated based on days impacted by repairs until completion. Manager team discusses with owner and processes as separate transaction, not deducted from monthly rent.
talking_points: Credits calculated by number of days property was uninhabitable
Recommended amount provided to owner for approval
Credit initiated as separate transaction, not deducted from rent payment
Tenant pays full rent; credit sent separately
how_to_request_rent_credit.suggested_response: We calculate rent credit requests based on the number of days you were impacted by repairs up until the completion date. The credit will be initiated as a separate transaction and will not be deducted from your monthly rent. You may proceed with the rent payment in full as usual.

how_to_cancel_rent_payment.answer: Once rent payment is initiated, the bank account cannot be switched. Payment can be cancelled before processing, and tenant must reinitiate from correct account.
talking_points: Cannot switch bank accounts after payment initiated
Can cancel payment before it fully processes
Tenant must log in and reinitiate from correct account
Recommend deleting accounts not intended for payment
how_to_cancel_rent_payment.suggested_response: We are unable to switch the accounts after the payment was initiated. However, we were able to cancel the payment as requested. Please log-in to Ziprent to initiate the payment from the bank account you want it arranged from.

how_to_update_property_insurance.answer: Owners submit updated insurance policies via email or portal. Manager team confirms receipt and updates records.
talking_points: Email insurance document to manager team
Team adds document to property files
Confirmation provided once uploaded
Should include policy effective dates
how_to_update_property_insurance.suggested_response: Thank you for providing us with your updated policy. We've added this document to your files.

how_to_add_utility_bill_to_tenant_portal.answer: Owners can add utility bills to tenant portal themselves or request manager team to upload. Bill details and amount must be provided.
talking_points: Owners can upload directly via portal under 'Rents' > 'Add Rent Bill'
Can request manager team to upload by providing bill copy and amount
Tenant notified once bill added to portal
Tenant initiates payment through portal
how_to_add_utility_bill_to_tenant_portal.suggested_response: Thank you for providing us with this utility bill. I've uploaded it to the tenant's portal for payment and will notify them of the charge. You can also add bills directly by logging into the portal, selecting 'Rents' from the left sidebar, then selecting 'Add Rent Bill'.

how_to_schedule_owner_or_vendor_visit.answer: Manager team requests tenant availability, coordinates scheduling between owner/vendor and tenant, and confirms appointment details.
talking_points: Manager team requests available dates/times from tenant
Coordinates with owner or vendor to match availability
Provides confirmation to all parties once scheduled
Includes access instructions when needed
how_to_schedule_owner_or_vendor_visit.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. We will check with the tenant and update you on their availability. Please let us know if there is anything else we can assist you with.

what_is_1099_tax_form_process.answer: Owners must provide W9/SSN/EIN information via portal or form. Tax forms can be generated in portal under Tax Forms section after information submitted.
talking_points: Final notice sent to owners requiring tax information
Can input info in portal (most secure) or fill attached W9
Access via email dropdown > Tax Forms
Select 'Add Tax Form' to generate for specific year
Previous year information reused if no changes made
what_is_1099_tax_form_process.suggested_response: If you require a 1099, please log into your Ziprent account, select 'Tax Forms' from the drop-down menu under your email address. Then select 'Add Tax Form' to generate a form. If you would like to use last year's information, you do not need to make any changes.

how_long_does_rent_deposit_take.answer: Rent deposits take 3-5 business days to process and show in owner's account. Holidays may extend processing time.
talking_points: Standard processing time is 3-5 business days
Holidays can extend processing time
Tenant initiates payment; processing begins after that
Owners notified when deposit initiated
how_long_does_rent_deposit_take.suggested_response: The tenant initiated rent payment on [date]. Please note that payments typically take 3–5 business days to reflect in your account. Due to [holiday], processing may take longer than usual.

how_to_verify_rental_history_for_tenant.answer: External landlords submit rental verification requests. Manager team confirms tenancy details including dates, rent amount, payment history, and lease compliance.
talking_points: Verify rental address and monthly rent
Confirm residency period
Report on payment history (NSF checks, late payments)
Confirm no unlawful detainer filings or outstanding balances
Note lease violations if any
how_to_verify_rental_history_for_tenant.suggested_response: Please see the completed rental verification below: - Rental address and monthly rent: Confirmed - Residency period: Confirmed [dates] - NSF checks: None - Unlawful detainer: None - Outstanding amounts: None - Lease violations: None - Would you rent to this applicant again: Yes

how_to_handle_hoa_violations.answer: Owners forward HOA violation notices to manager team, who relay to tenants with request to remedy. Follow-up required to confirm correction and inform HOA.
talking_points: Owner forwards HOA notice to manager team
Manager relays notice to tenant with deadline
Request tenant send photo proof of correction
Manager updates owner and/or HOA once resolved
Tenant must comply to avoid fines
how_to_handle_hoa_violations.suggested_response: The owner has provided us with the attached HOA notice regarding [violation]. Please review the details and keep us updated once this has been rectified so we may inform the owner. Please confirm that you will abide by HOA rules moving forward to avoid any possible penalties.

how_to_forward_hoa_announcements.answer: Owners forward HOA announcements (maintenance, inspections, events) to manager team, who relay to tenants with 'Please see below from HOA' formatting.
