talking_points: HOA notifications include maintenance, inspections, amenity updates
Owner forwards to manager@ziprent.com
Manager team forwards to tenant with HOA attribution
Covers parking updates, building work, utility shutoffs, community events
how_to_forward_hoa_announcements.suggested_response: Please see below from the HOA: [HOA message content]

how_to_waive_late_fees.answer: Owners can request late fees be waived or not charged. If already processed, refund issued once transaction clears. Grace period adjustments can be made in system.
talking_points: Owner must approve waiving late fee
If already charged, refund processed after clearing
Lease grace period can be adjusted in system if owner wants unofficial grace period
Late fees auto-apply based on lease terms unless waived
how_to_waive_late_fees.suggested_response: Thank you for letting us know you do not want to charge the late fee. We will arrange the refund once the transaction has fully cleared. Please note that if you prefer a grace period moving forward, we can adjust the system settings accordingly.

how_to_add_or_remove_pet.answer: Tenants provide pet details (breed, gender, weight, age, name). Owner reviews and approves. Pet addendum created and signed by all parties.
talking_points: Tenant submits pet information (breed, gender, weight, age, name)
Manager presents to owner for approval
Pet addendum generated upon approval
All parties must sign addendum
Pet deposit/fee may apply per lease terms
how_to_add_or_remove_pet.suggested_response: The tenants have confirmed the following information regarding the pet they will be adopting: Breed, Gender, Weight, Age, Name. Please let us know if you approve of this addition. We will then prepare the pet addendum for signature.

how_to_access_property_photos.answer: Property photos can be downloaded from owner's Ziprent portal. Located under property details or walkthrough tab.
talking_points: Log into Ziprent portal
Navigate to property details or walkthrough section
Download option available for photos
Move-in/move-out photos stored with walkthroughs
how_to_access_property_photos.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. You can download the photos from your Ziprent portal under the walkthrough tab or property details section.

how_to_contact_new_tenant.answer: Owners cannot directly contact tenants due to privacy. Can provide information via manager team, who acts as intermediary (furniture offers, move-in coordination).
talking_points: Cannot share tenant contact information directly
Manager team facilitates communication
Useful for furniture offers, access coordination, move-in instructions
Owner sends message/question to manager team who relays to tenant
how_to_contact_new_tenant.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. You can share the photos and details with us, and we will check with the new tenant if they are interested. We'll update you on their response.

how_to_process_rent_increase.answer: Owner provides 30-day notice to tenant with new rent amount. Manager team updates portal to reflect increase starting specified date.
talking_points: Owner must provide proper notice (typically 30 days)
Notice should specify new rent amount and effective date
Manager team updates system once notice provided
Can be implemented at renewal or mid-lease per local laws
Notice must be shared with manager team to update records
how_to_process_rent_increase.suggested_response: Thank you for bringing this to our attention. Please share the notice so we can update our records and adjust the rent amount accordingly starting [date].

how_to_cancel_property_management.answer: Owner requests cancellation effective specific date. Must provide alternative contact info for tenant communications. Transition facilitated by manager team.
talking_points: Owner specifies cancellation effective date
Must provide new property manager or owner contact details
Manager team notifies tenant of change as one-time courtesy
All future inquiries directed to new contact
Can transition to rent-processing-only service
how_to_cancel_property_management.suggested_response: We're happy to facilitate the transition from full property management services to [alternative]. Please provide the contact information to be relayed to the tenants for their future repair, rent collection and property management questions.

can_tenants_pay_rent_with_credit_card.answer: Portal only accepts ACH payments from checking/savings accounts. Credit cards like Bilt that can pay as ACH are acceptable.
talking_points: Portal accepts ACH payments only
Checking or savings accounts work
Some rent rewards cards (Bilt) can pay via ACH and are acceptable
Credit card must have ACH capability to work
can_tenants_pay_rent_with_credit_card.suggested_response: Payments are allowable as long as the account you are paying from can pay as an ACH. Our portal will only accept ACH payments. We do see tenants pay with BILT/Rent Rewards accounts, so this is acceptable.

how_to_request_owner_visit_to_property.answer: Owner requests visit through manager team. Manager coordinates with tenant for available dates/times. Must provide proper notice per lease (typically 24-48 hours).
talking_points: Owner submits visit request with purpose and preferred dates
Manager team requests tenant availability
Proper notice provided per lease terms
Coordination for showing, inspection, or appraisal
Confirmation sent once scheduled
how_to_request_owner_visit_to_property.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. Please provide us with a few dates and times that work best for you so we may coordinate with the tenant accordingly.

how_to_update_bank_account_information.answer: Owners update bank account in portal under payment settings. Manager team can assist with adding account or verifying account shows correctly.
talking_points: Log into portal and navigate to payment/banking section
Can add new account or update existing
Manager team can verify account is linked correctly
Micro-deposits may be required to verify new accounts
Confirm account number (last 4 digits) with manager team
how_to_update_bank_account_information.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. We see the new account linked to your property, which ends with (x[digits]). Please let us know if you need any further assistance.

what_to_do_about_prospect_inquiry.answer: Manager team forwards prospect questions about property to owner. Owner provides answer, manager relays to prospect and updates property notes.
talking_points: Questions about parking, storage, amenities, utilities
Owner provides accurate information
Manager updates prospect and property records
Helps prospects make informed showing/application decisions
what_to_do_about_prospect_inquiry.suggested_response: A prospect has reached out and inquired the following: [question]. We are looking forward to your response to share with the prospect and to update our records for future reference.

how_does_lease_break_policy_work.answer: Tenants responsible for full lease term but can request early termination. Ziprent locates new tenant for $1,250 fee (or two months rent if higher). Tenant must cooperate with showings.
talking_points: Tenant fully responsible for rent during entire lease term
Must notify intent to break lease in writing
Ziprent will locate, vet, and sign new tenant
Cost is $1,250 or two months rent (whichever higher)
Tenant must cooperate with showings
Still liable for rent until new tenant moves in
how_does_lease_break_policy_work.suggested_response: While tenants are fully responsible for rent during the entire lease term, we understand unforeseen circumstances may arise. Should you need to vacate, Ziprent will locate, vet and sign a new lease for the premises at a cost of $1,250 (or two months rent if higher). You must cooperate with showings and remain responsible for rent until a new tenant is secured.

how_to_change_rent_due_date.answer: Owner requests due date change, typically handled at lease renewal. Renewals team adjusts lease terms to reflect new due date.
talking_points: Due date changes typically done at renewal
Owner submits request to manager/renewals team
New lease reflects updated due date
Prorated charges may apply for transition month
how_to_change_rent_due_date.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. We see that you have communicated this request with our Renewals Team, and they will get back to you as soon as possible. Updates will be provided in the related email thread to avoid confusion.

can_i_stop_rent_reminder_emails.answer: Rent reminder emails are automatically sent to all leaseholders and cannot be disabled. They are part of standard tenant communication.
talking_points: Reminders automatically sent to all leaseholders
Part of standard rent collection process
Cannot be turned off for individual tenants
Sent at consistent intervals before due date
can_i_stop_rent_reminder_emails.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. Please note that these reminders are automatically sent to all leaseholders and cannot be disabled.

how_to_report_property_damage_or_issue.answer: Tenants submit repair requests through portal or email repairs@ziprent.com. If portal issues, manager team can create ticket manually with description and photos.
talking_points: Primary method: submit via Ziprent portal
Alternative: email repairs@ziprent.com
If portal not working, manager team creates ticket manually
Include detailed description and photos
Job number provided for tracking
how_to_report_property_damage_or_issue.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. We apologize for the portal issue. We have initiated a repair ticket with Job ID: [number]. Our repair team will follow up with you shortly.

what_if_rent_is_marked_paid_incorrectly.answer: If owner marked rent 'paid externally' by mistake, they should contact manager team immediately. Can be corrected if payment not actually received.
talking_points: Owner can mark rent paid outside system
If done incorrectly, contact manager team immediately
System will show paid and not send reminders
Manager team can investigate and correct
Clarify if payment actually received or error
what_if_rent_is_marked_paid_incorrectly.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. We see that you marked the tenants' rent as paid externally on [date]. Please let us know if this was done by mistake and you haven't actually received the payment yet.

how_to_request_property_management_fee_d.answer: Tenant placement fee is standard $1,250. Multi-property or repeat customers can request review, but discounts are not automatic.
talking_points: Standard tenant placement fee is $1,250
Request can be submitted for long-term/repeat clients
Not automatically discounted
Manager team reviews on case-by-case basis
how_to_request_property_management_fee_d.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. We understand your request for a discount on the tenant placement fee. While our standard rates reflect the services we provide, we will look into this and get back to you with an update.

what_is_returned_rent_fee.answer: Returned rent fee charged when bank returns payment due to NSF or other reason. Fee covers bank charges and cannot be waived. Tenant should contact bank.
talking_points: Charged when bank returns transaction
Common reason: insufficient funds (NSF)
Fee covers cost bank charges Ziprent
Cannot be waived as it's actual cost incurred
Tenant should contact bank with questions about return
what_is_returned_rent_fee.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. We see that your bank returned the transaction due to insufficient funds. We recommend contacting your bank with any questions. Please note that we are unable to waive the returned fee as this covers the cost the bank charges when a transaction has been returned.

how_to_transition_utilities_to_tenant.answer: Can be implemented at renewal or via addendum with tenant consent. Owner notifies manager team of change; tenant responsible for setup.
talking_points: Change requires tenant consent
Can be done at renewal or via addendum
Owner notifies manager team of change
Tenant responsible for contacting utility companies
May affect rent amount if utilities previously included
how_to_transition_utilities_to_tenant.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. This change can be implemented either at the time of lease renewal or through an addendum. Please note that tenant consent will be required. Let us know how you would like to proceed.

how_to_delete_ziprent_account.answer: Users can request account deletion by emailing manager team. Information removed from system upon request.
talking_points: Submit deletion request to manager@ziprent.com
Account information removed from system
Confirmation provided once completed
how_to_delete_ziprent_account.suggested_response: Your information has been removed from our system.

what_is_operations_invoice_5_percent_fee.answer: 5% processing fee applies to all invoices processed through Ziprent for services, repairs, or transactions.
talking_points: Standard 5% fee on all invoices
Covers processing and payment handling
Applied to repairs, turnovers, operations charges
Disclosed in service terms
what_is_operations_invoice_5_percent_fee.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. This is the 5% processing fee that is associated with all invoices processed through Ziprent.

how_to_become_ziprent_vendor.answer: Vendors complete profile questionnaire with service types, areas served, and contact info. Vendor relations team reviews and sets up profile.
talking_points: Fill out vendor registration form
Provide services offered, service areas, contact details
Vendor relations team reviews submission
Contact made once profile approved
Can email vendors@ziprent.com with questions
how_to_become_ziprent_vendor.suggested_response: We appreciate your interest in becoming a vendor with us. Please fill out this vendor questionnaire so our vendor relations team can set up your profile and contact you: [link]

how_are_partial_rent_payments_handled.answer: Portal cannot accept partial rent payments. Full amount must be paid at once. Owner can approve payment plan but tenant must initiate full amount when able.
talking_points: System does not support partial payments
Full rent amount must be initiated at once
Owner can approve later payment date
Late fees still apply per lease terms
how_are_partial_rent_payments_handled.suggested_response: Unfortunately, our portal is not equipped to take partial payments for rent. You will need to initiate the full payment as soon as you can. Please confirm if we should inform the homeowner that you will initiate the full rent amount on [date].

how_to_request_hoa_documents.answer: Owners submit HOA documents to manager team, who upload to portal. Documents provided to tenants as needed (handbook, rules, parking policies).
talking_points: Owner provides HOA documents to manager team
Documents uploaded to property files
Available to tenants as needed
Includes CC&Rs, rules, parking policies, handbooks
how_to_request_hoa_documents.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. We will reach out to the owner to request a copy of the HOA handbook and will keep you updated once we receive it.

how_to_schedule_lockbox_pickup.answer: Owner confirms lockbox location and readiness. Manager team schedules inspector to pick up. Lockbox should be accessible without entry.
talking_points: Owner removes lockbox from door and places in accessible location
Notify manager team when ready for pickup
Inspector scheduled to retrieve
Include photo of location if needed
how_to_schedule_lockbox_pickup.suggested_response: Thank you for the update. An inspector has been scheduled to pick up the lockbox.

how_to_handle_showing_no_shows.answer: After showing time, owner receives email to confirm which scheduled attendees actually showed up. Used for tracking and follow-up.
talking_points: System sends confirmation request after showing
Owner selects who actually attended from list
Helps track serious prospects
Assists with follow-up on applications
how_to_handle_showing_no_shows.suggested_response: Thank you for the update. We have updated our records accordingly.

how_to_confirm_tenant_application_receiv.answer: Manager team confirms application is complete and directs applicant to applications team for processing and follow-up.
talking_points: Manager team can verify application received
Applications team handles processing and communication
Contact applications@ziprent.com or (415) 843-5973
Keep eye out for applications team emails
how_to_confirm_tenant_application_receiv.suggested_response: We see that your application is complete. Please keep an eye out for our applications team to get in touch with you. If you have any queries regarding your application, please reach out to our applications team at applications@ziprent.com or (415) 843-5973.

what_if_owner_wants_to_sell_property.answer: Owner notifies manager team of intent to sell. Can offer property to current tenant first. Showings coordinated with tenant cooperation.
talking_points: Owner informs manager team of selling plans
Option to notify tenant and offer first opportunity
Showings scheduled through manager coordination
Tenant must allow reasonable showing access per lease
what_if_owner_wants_to_sell_property.suggested_response: Please let us know if you would like us to share your intentions of selling the property and ask if the tenant is interested in purchasing.

how_to_generate_1099_in_portal.answer: Landlords can generate their 1099 by logging into the Ziprent portal, clicking their email at the top right, selecting 'Tax Forms' from the dropdown, then clicking 'Add Tax Form' or 'Generate' to create and download the PDF.
talking_points: Login at app.ziprent.com/auth/login
Click email address in top right corner and select 'Tax Forms' from dropdown menu
Click 'Add Tax Form' on right side or 'Generate' button under Actions
Fill out required information (Name, SSN/EIN, Address) if not already completed
Sign and click 'Store' to generate the PDF for download
how_to_generate_1099_in_portal.suggested_response: To create your tax form, please log into your Ziprent account at https://app.ziprent.com/auth/login and select 'Tax Forms' from the drop-down menu under your email address (located at the top right side). Then click 'Add Tax Form' on the right hand side to generate your form. Once you select 'Submit' this will generate a PDF of your tax form for download. Please let us know if you need any further assistance.

how_to_split_1099_between_co_owners.answer: Co-owners can split a 1099 equally by both submitting their tax information in their respective portals. After signing and clicking 'Store', a popup will ask if they want to split the income equally among all owners - select 'Yes'.
talking_points: Each owner must log into their own Ziprent portal and submit tax information
After completing the form and adding signature, a window appears asking about splitting
Select 'Yes' to split the 1099 equally amongst all owners
Both owners will receive separate 1099s with the income divided equally
This option only appears after selecting 'Store' on the signature page
how_to_split_1099_between_co_owners.suggested_response: Yes, we can split the 1099 equally amongst owners. Each owner will be required to submit their tax info through their own portal. Once you request the 1099 and complete the signing process, the option to split the 1099 across all owners equally will be provided within a popup window. Please select 'Yes' when prompted. Let us know if you have any questions!

why_cant_i_select_property_checkbox.answer: If a property cannot be selected when generating a 1099, it's usually because a co-owner has already generated a 1099 for that property, or there's a system bug related to the tax year selection.
talking_points: Property checkboxes become unavailable after someone else has already claimed the income
Check with co-owners to see if they've already generated the 1099
Sometimes caused by system bug defaulting to wrong tax year
Try logging out and back in, or try 'Generate' button instead of checkbox selection
Contact support if issue persists after troubleshooting
why_cant_i_select_property_checkbox.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. A property can no longer be selected when the income has been applied to someone else's 1099. Please check with the other people you share this property with, as they may have already claimed the income on their 1099. If this doesn't resolve the issue, there may be a system bug - please let us know and we'll investigate further.

can_ziprent_mail_physical_1099.answer: Ziprent does not mail physical copies of 1099 forms. All forms are available for download electronically through the owner portal.
talking_points: 1099 forms are only available electronically through the portal
No physical copies are mailed to owners
Owners can download PDF copies anytime from their account
If address is required for foreign owners, use the rental property address instead
can_ziprent_mail_physical_1099.suggested_response: As we do not mail a copy of the 1099, all forms are available electronically through your portal for download. You can access and print your 1099 at any time by logging into your Ziprent account and navigating to the Tax Forms section.

what_if_ssn_or_info_hasnt_changed.answer: Even if SSN and information haven't changed from the previous year, owners still need to generate the new year's 1099 through the portal. The system will reuse previous information but requires annual generation.
talking_points: The system will automatically reuse the same information from last year
However, the 1099 must still be generated for the new tax year
If not generated manually, the system will auto-generate by February 15th using prior info
Automated reminder emails will continue until the form is generated
This is an IRS requirement - new forms must be filed each year
what_if_ssn_or_info_hasnt_changed.suggested_response: The system will re-use the same information as last year. However, please note that if you do not submit and generate the 1099 for this year, the system will continue to send reminders until it auto-generates itself. The 1099s need to be submitted to the IRS each year, even if the information hasn't changed.

why_am_i_still_getting_reminder_emails.answer: Reminder emails continue until the 1099 is fully generated (not just information entered). The final step is clicking 'Generate' and 'Store' to complete the process. All co-owners listed on the property receive reminders via BCC.
talking_points: Entering information is not enough - must click 'Generate' and 'Store' to complete
Automated reminders continue until the 1099 is fully generated
All owners listed on the portal receive reminder emails via BCC
Once one owner completes the process, reminders should stop
After generation, please disregard any automated follow-ups
why_am_i_still_getting_reminder_emails.suggested_response: Thank you for confirming this has been completed. Your 1099 is now available in the portal and no further action is needed. I apologize for any automated follow-ups you may receive - these can be disregarded. The system sends reminders until the form is fully generated, which has now been completed.

how_to_correct_1099_errors.answer: For 1099 corrections (wrong SSN, name, or amount), contact the manager team. They can issue corrected 1099s and set incorrect ones to zero, though forms cannot be moved between email accounts.
talking_points: Contact manager@ziprent.com with specific correction details
Corrections can be issued for amounts, SSN, name, or address
Previous incorrect 1099s can be set to $0
Forms cannot be transferred between different email accounts
Corrected forms will be available in the portal under the correct email
how_to_correct_1099_errors.suggested_response: A correction has been issued for the total amount, and you can find it under your email in the portal. A correction was also issued for the previous 1099 to set the earnings to 0. Unfortunately, we cannot move forms between different email accounts. Please let us know if you need any additional corrections.

what_if_1099_already_completed.answer: If a 1099 is already complete and showing in the portal, owners can disregard final notice emails. The form is ready for download and no further action is needed.
talking_points: Check portal under Tax Forms section to confirm completion
Blue download button should be visible under Actions
Automated reminders may continue briefly after completion
Any further reminder emails can be disregarded
Form remains available for download at any time
what_if_1099_already_completed.suggested_response: I see your 1099 is complete and available in the portal. I apologize for the automated follow-up - this can be disregarded. Your form is ready for download under the Tax Forms section whenever you need it. Thank you for confirming!

can_1099_be_generated_under_trust_or_llc.answer: Yes, 1099s can be generated under a trust or LLC name. Use the trust/LLC name and EIN instead of individual name and SSN. For specific questions about which entity to use, consult a tax professional or attorney.
talking_points: Use the legal entity name (trust or LLC) in the business name field
Use the entity's EIN instead of personal SSN
For trusts, the trustee typically generates the form under the trust name
Ziprent cannot provide tax or legal advice on which entity to use
Consult with a tax professional or attorney for entity-specific questions
can_1099_be_generated_under_trust_or_llc.suggested_response: You can generate the 1099 under your trust or LLC name by using the entity's legal name and EIN in the form. However, I apologize but we cannot provide specific guidance on whether to use your name as trustee or the trust name itself - you may need to check this with an attorney or tax professional. Please let us know if you have any other questions!

what_if_multiple_properties_under_differ.answer: If properties are owned by different LLCs, separate 1099s must be generated for each LLC. Each LLC should be added as an owner in the portal with its own EIN, then generate separate forms for each entity.
talking_points: Different LLCs require separate 1099s with different EINs
Add each LLC as an owner entity in the Ziprent dashboard if not already present
Generate separate tax forms for each LLC/property combination
Ensure correct properties are associated with correct LLC when generating
Contact support if LLC entities need to be added to the dashboard
what_if_multiple_properties_under_differ.suggested_response: For properties under separate LLCs, you'll need to generate separate 1099s for each entity. The LLC that is not currently showing in your dashboard will need to be added so we can process the forms properly. Once that's added, you can generate individual tax forms for each LLC with the correct EIN and property selection. Can you add that entity or would you like us to assist?

what_if_co_owner_doesnt_want_1099.answer: All owners listed on the portal receive 1099 reminder emails. If someone doesn't receive income, they don't need to generate a form, but they'll continue receiving reminders until the actual income recipient completes their 1099.
talking_points: Reminder emails are sent via BCC to all listed owners
Only the person receiving income needs to generate the 1099
Reminders cannot be turned off individually for co-owners
Once the income recipient generates their form, reminders should stop for everyone
Non-receiving owners can ignore reminder emails
what_if_co_owner_doesnt_want_1099.suggested_response: Everyone listed as owners within the portal receives these reminder emails via BCC. Until the owner who receives the income submits their tax document and generates their 1099, all listed owners will receive these reminders. We are unable to turn off these notifications individually. You can safely disregard these emails if you are not the one receiving the rental income.

what_ein_format_is_required.answer: EINs must be in the format XX-XXXXXXX (9 digits with hyphen). If the number provided is too long or in the wrong format, the system will return an error.
talking_points: EIN format is XX-XXXXXXX (two digits, hyphen, seven digits)
Total of 9 numeric digits
System will reject numbers that are too long or improperly formatted
SSNs follow format XXX-XX-XXXX
Remove any extra digits or characters
what_ein_format_is_required.suggested_response: The number provided is too long to be an EIN. Please note the EIN should be in the following format: XX-XXXXXXX (9 digits total with a hyphen after the first two digits). Please verify your EIN and try entering it again in this format.

what_if_i_live_outside_us.answer: For owners living outside the US, use the rental property address in the address field since Ziprent doesn't mail physical copies. Non-US residents may need to complete Form W-8BEN instead of W-9.
talking_points: Use the US rental property address in the address field
Physical forms are not mailed, so foreign address isn't critical
For owners who moved overseas, Form W-8BEN may be required instead
W-8BEN form can be downloaded from IRS website and returned to Ziprent
Contact support for specific guidance on international tax situations
what_if_i_live_outside_us.suggested_response: Since you're living outside the US and we do not mail physical copies of the 1099, please add your rental property address instead in the address field. Additionally, if you've moved overseas permanently, you may need to complete a Form W-8BEN instead. You can download it here: https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-pdf/fw8ben.pdf. Please let us know if you have any questions!

what_does_1099_amount_include.answer: The 1099 amount includes all rental income that Ziprent collected and disbursed on the owner's behalf during the tax year. It does not include security deposits, and only reflects income processed through the Ziprent system.
talking_points: Includes all rent collected by Ziprent during the tax year
May include prorated rent for partial months
Does not include security deposits
Only includes income processed through Ziprent's system
For tenant-placement-only properties, only covers rent during Ziprent management period
Owners must separately report any rent collected outside of Ziprent
what_does_1099_amount_include.suggested_response: The amount shown on your 1099 represents all rental income that Ziprent collected on your behalf during the tax year. This does not include any security deposits. Ziprent is required to report all income we collected to the IRS. If you collected additional rent outside of our system, you would report that separately. The amount of [specific amount] is accurate based on what we processed through our platform.

how_to_get_profit_and_loss_statement.answer: Profit and Loss statements are accessed the same way as 1099s - through the Tax Forms section of the portal. The statement shows income and expenses but requires selecting the property checkbox to generate.
talking_points: Login to portal and go to Tax Forms section
Same process as generating 1099
Must select property checkbox to generate P&L statement
Shows rental income and property management fees/expenses
Available as PDF download with Ziprent branding
Contact support if unable to select property checkbox
how_to_get_profit_and_loss_statement.suggested_response: You can access your Profit and Loss statement through the same Tax Forms section in your portal. Log in, select 'Tax Forms' from the dropdown menu under your email, and follow the same steps as generating a 1099. Make sure to select the property checkbox when prompted. If you're having trouble with the checkbox, please let us know and we can assist further.

what_if_management_fees_wrong_on_1099.answer: If property management fees or expenses appear incorrect on the 1099, the team will investigate and can issue corrections. Contact manager@ziprent.com with specific details about the discrepancy.
talking_points: Management fees should reflect actual PM fees paid during the tax year
Team will review account to verify correct amounts
Corrections can be issued if fees were not properly included
Provide specific amounts expected vs. what's shown
Response typically provided after investigation
what_if_management_fees_wrong_on_1099.suggested_response: We have checked your account and are looking into why the management fees shown don't match your records. We see [amount] of PM fees were paid during the tax year for your property. We are investigating why this wasn't properly reflected and will keep you updated on the correction. Thank you for bringing this to our attention.

when_is_1099_deadline.answer: The deadline for submitting 1099 information is typically late January (around January 29th). If owners don't submit by February 15th, Ziprent will auto-generate using previous year's information.
talking_points: Initial deadline is usually late January
Final notice emails sent around January 21-22
Auto-generation happens by February 15th if not completed
Uses previous year's information for auto-generation if available
IRS filing deadline requires timely submission
when_is_1099_deadline.suggested_response: The deadline to submit your 1099 information is January 29th. If we do not receive an updated submission from you by February 15th, we will use last year's information if one was submitted. Please complete your form as soon as possible to ensure accuracy. Let us know if you need any assistance!

can_i_provide_w9_by_email_or_attachment.answer: While W-9 forms can be provided by email attachment in some cases, the preferred and most secure method is entering the information directly through the Ziprent portal dashboard.
talking_points: Portal entry is the most secure method
Email attachments accepted in some circumstances
For contractors/vendors, email may be the only option
For property owners, portal entry is strongly preferred
Team will confirm receipt of emailed W-9s

While we can accept it via email, the most secure method is to input your information directly into our portal at https://app.ziprent.com/auth/login. If you've already emailed it, we'll process it and confirm receipt. Please let us know if you have any questions!

what_if_need_ziprent_w9_for_my_taxes.answer: Landlords needing Ziprent's W-9 or EIN for their own tax filing (when rent is paid to Ziprent) can request it from the manager team, who will provide the company's TIN and legal business information.
talking_points: Some landlords need Ziprent's W-9 if they issue 1099s to Ziprent
Typically needed when rent payments go directly to Ziprent first
Manager team can provide Ziprent's Federal EIN
Legal business name and TIN information will be shared
Usually needed for S-Corp owners or certain business structures

We see that we were able to provide you with our TIN information. Feel free to reach out if you have any questions or need additional details about our company information for your records.

how_to_handle_trust_as_owner.answer: For trusts, the 1099 should typically be generated under the trust name using the trust's EIN. However, specific trust tax questions should be directed to a tax professional or attorney.
talking_points: Use the full legal trust name as it appears on trust documents
Use the trust's EIN, not the trustee's personal SSN
Ziprent cannot provide legal advice on trust tax matters

Please consult with a tax professional or attorney for guidance specific to your trust structure.

what_if_only_partial_year_management.answer: If Ziprent only managed the property for part of the year, the 1099 will only include the rental income collected during the period Ziprent was managing. Owners must report other income collected outside Ziprent separately.

Partial year management means partial year income on 1099
Owner responsible for reporting income collected directly

1099 amount will be less than full year's rent if partial management

A tax professional can assist you with combining income from multiple sources.

how_to_handle_c_corp_or_business_entity.answer: C-Corps and other business entities should use their legal business name and EIN when generating 1099s. The entity type should be indicated when submitting tax information.

Business entities follow same portal process as individuals
W-9 should match business registration documents

Look forward to their updates after the visit

Indicate you'll reach out for the next task
Maintain positive relationship for future assignments

If the issue persists, please submit a repair request through your Ziprent portal with detailed information and photos. We will coordinate repairs promptly.

can_owner_handle_repairs_themselves.answer: Owners can handle repairs directly but must coordinate access with tenants and update management on completion.
talking_points: Owners may use their own contractors/handyman
Owners must coordinate scheduling directly with tenants
Management should be notified of completion
Owners should update repair threads with status
Tenants must provide reasonable access
Management cancels Ziprent vendor bids if owner handles repair
Photos/documentation of completed work is helpful
can_owner_handle_repairs_themselves.suggested_response: Thank you for letting us know. Please coordinate scheduling directly with the tenants for access to the property. Once the repairs are complete, please confirm with us and we will notify the tenants and close out any related repair tickets. We appreciate you keeping us updated.

what_are_normal_wear_and_tear_items.answer: Minor scratches, chips, scuffs, and small marks are normal wear and tear and should be marked 'OK' not 'Bad'. 'Bad' is for items needing immediate repair.
talking_points: Normal wear includes: minor scratches, small chips, light scuffs, minor marks
These should be marked 'OK' not 'Bad' in walkthrough
'Bad' is reserved for items requiring immediate repair
'Bad' indicates habitability or safety concerns
Normal wear doesn't warrant security deposit deductions
Document existing wear to protect security deposit
Management may request amended report if normal wear marked as 'Bad'
what_are_normal_wear_and_tear_items.suggested_response: We've noticed that several areas have been marked as 'Bad' due to scratches, chips, and minor marks. These types of issues are considered normal wear and tear and would typically be classified as 'OK' rather than 'Bad.' The 'Bad' category should be reserved for issues that require immediate repair and impact the habitability or safety of the property. Could you please review and amend the report accordingly?

where_to_find_mailbox_key.answer: Mailbox keys are typically with other keys in kitchen cabinets or lockbox. Some properties don't require keys (combination mailboxes).
talking_points: Check kitchen cabinets/drawers where other keys are located
May be in same lockbox as house keys
Some mailboxes use combination locks and don't require keys
Owner can confirm mailbox number and key location
Management can request duplicate if key is missing
Square-shaped keys typically open pool/amenity areas, not mailboxes
where_to_find_mailbox_key.suggested_response: The mailbox key should be located with the other keys in the kitchen cabinet/drawer. If you cannot locate it, please let us know and we will confirm the mailbox key location with the owner. Please also confirm your mailbox number so we can ensure you have the correct key.

what_if_property_needs_cleaning_at_move_.answer: Submit repair request for move-in cleaning. Include specific areas and photos. Management coordinates professional cleaning through vendor bids.
talking_points: Submit cleaning request through repair portal
Include specific areas needing attention and photos
Professional cleaning vendors will be coordinated
Management collects bids for owner approval
Move-in cleaning is typically owner responsibility
Deep cleaning should be completed before move-in
Document uncleaned areas to protect security deposit
what_if_property_needs_cleaning_at_move_.suggested_response: Please submit a repair request through your Ziprent portal for cleaning. Include detailed descriptions of the areas needing attention and photos. We will coordinate with professional cleaning vendors and collect bids for the owner's approval. Move-in cleaning is important and should be addressed promptly.

how_to_reach_repairs_team_for_open_ticke.answer: Reply directly to repair ticket emails or contact repairs@ziprent.com. Include job ID number for specific repairs.
talking_points: Reply to repair ticket email threads for updates on that specific job
Email repairs@ziprent.com for general repair inquiries
Include job ID number (e.g., Job #12345) in communications
Phone: 415-843-5964 or (408) 638-4190 for urgent matters
Manager team handles walkthroughs; repairs team handles active tickets
Check portal for repair status updates
One repair thread can address multiple related items
how_to_reach_repairs_team_for_open_ticke.suggested_response: We see that our repairs team is actively addressing this issue in the related repair thread. For any updates or inquiries regarding this specific repair ticket, please reach out to our repairs team at repairs@ziprent.com or reply directly to the repair ticket email thread. This ensures faster response on active repairs.

what_is_owner_vs_self_managed_property.answer: Some owners self-manage repairs. For self-managed properties, tenants contact owner directly for repairs rather than Ziprent.
talking_points: Self-managed properties: owner handles all repairs directly
Tenants contact owner directly for repair requests
Ziprent still handles lease administration and walkthroughs
Owner's contact information provided for self-managed properties
Fully managed properties: Ziprent coordinates all repairs
Walkthrough still required for documentation purposes
what_is_owner_vs_self_managed_property.suggested_response: As the owner has selected to self-manage repairs, you will need to contact the owner directly for any maintenance requests. We have provided the owner's contact information. Ziprent will continue to handle lease administration and documentation.

what_if_tenant_has_only_one_key.answer: Check all key locations (lockboxes, kitchen). One key may be in emergency lockbox. Tenant can make duplicates for reimbursement or owner can provide additional key.
talking_points: Check primary lockbox and kitchen cabinets thoroughly
One key may be secured in emergency lockbox for owner/vendor access
Emergency lockbox typically on gas or water meter
Tenant can make duplicates and submit receipt for reimbursement
Owner can drop off additional key if available
Most properties should have 2+ house keys for tenants
what_if_tenant_has_only_one_key.suggested_response: The other key that was provided was placed inside a combo lockbox on the gas meter. We keep this lockbox at the property for owner and vendors in the event of an emergency. If you would like another property key, we recommend making a duplicate and providing a receipt for reimbursement, or the owner can drop off an additional key if available.

what_is_purpose_of_walkthrough.answer: Walkthrough documents property condition at move-in to serve as reference point at move-out and protect tenant's security deposit.
talking_points: Documents exact condition tenant received the property
Serves as reference point for move-out inspection
Protects tenant's security deposit from unfair deductions
Helps distinguish pre-existing issues from tenant-caused damage
Stored in system for duration of tenancy
Benefits both tenant and owner with clear documentation
More photos and detail = better protection
what_is_purpose_of_walkthrough.suggested_response: The walkthrough is highly beneficial, as it adequately documents the state in which you received the property. This will stay in our system and will serve as a point of reference for the shape of this property in the future. It can be used to dispute any deductions from your security deposit upon moving out. Please submit this at your earliest convenience with as many photos as possible.

can_i_schedule_walkthrough_or_inspection.answer: Tenants complete walkthroughs on their own. For issues needing owner visit, coordinate scheduling directly between owner and tenant.
talking_points: Tenants conduct their own move-in walkthrough
No Ziprent representative attends tenant walkthrough
Inspector walkthrough completed during onboarding (before tenant)
If owner/vendor needs access for repairs, coordinate directly with tenant
Tenant must provide reasonable access for repairs
Notice requirements apply for owner visits
Emergency repairs may have different access rules
can_i_schedule_walkthrough_or_inspection.suggested_response: Tenants complete the move-in walkthrough independently using the online form. If the owner needs to visit the property for repairs or inspection, please coordinate scheduling directly with the tenants. Tenants are required to provide reasonable access for necessary repairs and maintenance.

add_additional_owner.answer: Additional owners can be added by providing their full name and contact phone number to the onboarding team.
talking_points: Collect full name of additional owner
Collect contact phone number
Update system to include them in communications
add_additional_owner.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. Yes, we will be able to add [Name] as an additional property owner. Please provide us with their full name and contact phone number so we can update our records.

schedule_listing_appointment.answer: Listing appointments involve scheduling a photographer for property photos, inspection, and lockbox placement at a mutually convenient time.
talking_points: Coordinate owner and photographer availability
Photography includes inspection and lockbox setup
Confirm appointment once both parties agree on date/time
schedule_listing_appointment.suggested_response: Thank you for completing the questionnaire! The next step is to schedule the Listing Appointment. We've reached out to the photographer to find out their soonest availability and will circle back with you to confirm a time/day.

reschedule_appointment.answer: Appointments can be rescheduled by contacting the onboarding team, who will coordinate new availability between owner and photographer/vendor.
talking_points: Cancel existing appointment
Coordinate new availability
Send confirmation of rescheduled time
reschedule_appointment.suggested_response: Please note that the Photography, Inspection, and Lockbox Placement previously scheduled have been canceled. Please reach out with your updated availability so we can reschedule at your earliest convenience.

update_listing_description.answer: Listing descriptions can be corrected or updated by contacting the onboarding team with the specific changes needed.
talking_points: Note specific text to be changed
Update listing immediately
Confirm changes with owner
update_listing_description.suggested_response: I will update that information immediately. Thank you for bringing this to our attention and have a great day!

provide_lockbox_information.answer: Lockbox codes, locations, and key details should be provided to the onboarding team for access coordination and emergency purposes.
talking_points: Document lockbox location and code
Note which keys are inside
Include photos if possible for system records
provide_lockbox_information.suggested_response: Thank you for the lockbox information. We have updated our system with the location, code, and key details. If possible, please share a photo of the lockbox so we can update our records.

adjust_listing_price.answer: Rental prices can be adjusted through the owner portal or by contacting the onboarding team to update the listing amount.
talking_points: Confirm desired new price
Update listing across all platforms
Acknowledge price adjustment confirmation
adjust_listing_price.suggested_response: Thank you for updating the price. We have confirmed the listing is now set at [new amount]. Reducing the rent price is the most effective way to immediately increase responsiveness to your property.

complete_onboarding_questionnaire.answer: The onboarding questionnaire must be completed through the provided portal link with property details, lease information, and owner preferences.
talking_points: Share questionnaire link
Ensure all required fields are completed
Upload necessary documents like existing leases
complete_onboarding_questionnaire.suggested_response: We will require the property's information by completing the onboarding questionnaire. Please fill it out through this link: [questionnaire URL]. Once completed, we can proceed with the next steps.

provide_key_access.answer: Keys should be placed in designated lockboxes or arrangements made for key handoff to photographers/vendors for property access.
talking_points: Confirm key location and access method
Coordinate timing for key pickup if needed
Ensure keys are available before scheduled appointments
provide_key_access.suggested_response: Please confirm where keys will be available for our photographer. If you can place them in the lockbox beforehand, please provide the code. Otherwise, we can coordinate a key handoff time.

address_walkthrough_issues.answer: After walkthrough inspection, any required or recommended repairs must be addressed before listing the property or tenant move-in.
talking_points: Identify required vs. recommended items
Set timeline for completion
Follow up once items are addressed before proceeding
address_walkthrough_issues.suggested_response: Just reaching out regarding the walkthrough completed on the property. We have noted the following required items that need attention before listing. Please let us know when these are addressed so we can proceed.

upload_existing_lease.answer: Existing tenant leases should be uploaded through the owner portal under the Leases tab by clicking 'Upload Existing Lease'.
talking_points: Log into owner portal
Navigate to property and Leases tab
Upload PDF of complete signed lease
upload_existing_lease.suggested_response: Kindly upload the lease for your current tenant by logging into the portal. Once logged in, select the property address, navigate to the 'Leases' tab, and click on 'Upload Existing Lease'.

contact_prospective_tenant.answer: Ziprent will contact prospective tenants who need application assistance and provide them with the application link and guidance.
talking_points: Reach out via phone and text
Send application link directly
Follow up and update owner on response
contact_prospective_tenant.suggested_response: Thank you for the information. I have reached out to your prospective tenant and left a voicemail. I have also sent them a text message with a link to the application. We will update you as soon as we have heard back!

property_not_rent_ready.answer: Properties must be fully cleaned and repairs completed before photography and listing appointments can proceed.
talking_points: Confirm all cleaning and repairs are complete
Address any visible issues that could deter applicants
Reschedule if property not ready
property_not_rent_ready.suggested_response: Thank you for sending the photos. Is it possible to have [specific issue] addressed before the appointment? Each prospect is a potential renter and we have one chance to get them to apply, so you want all areas of the property looking their best.

listing_performance_feedback.answer: Listing performance data including lead count, showings scheduled, and applications initiated is tracked and can be provided upon request.
talking_points: Review lead and showing statistics
Identify potential issues with price or presentation
Provide recommendations for improvement
listing_performance_feedback.suggested_response: Upon review, your property has received [X] leads and had [Y] showings scheduled with [Z] applications initiated. We follow up with all prospects who engage with the listing. If you'd like to discuss strategies to increase interest, please let us know.

furnished_property_policy.answer: Ziprent does not list furnished properties; furniture must be removed before listing, or owner must handle showings until furniture is removed.
talking_points: Confirm Ziprent policy on furnished listings
Suggest owner-hosted showings if still furnished
Require furniture removal before tenant move-in
furnished_property_policy.suggested_response: Please be advised that we do not list furnished properties. If the property is furnished, we highly suggest that the owner host the showings and confirm that the owner will remove the furniture prior to a tenant's move-in.

cancel_services.answer: We offer flexible service cancellation through our online dashboard or by direct communication with your dedicated property management team.
talking_points: Cancellation can be initiated via the Ziprent dashboard under 'Account Settings'
Your assigned property manager can also process a cancellation request
Any outstanding fees or contractual obligations will be reviewed during the cancellation process
Processing time for cancellations typically takes 5-7 business days
cancel_services.suggested_response: I understand you're looking to cancel our services. I can definitely help you with that today. We want to make this process as smooth as possible for you, so I'll walk you through the steps and ensure all of your questions are answered.
cancel_services.source_title: FAQ: How do I cancel services?

photographer_availability_scheduling.answer: Photographer availability is coordinated based on owner's provided time windows, with the onboarding team confirming appointments once both parties' schedules align.
talking_points: Collect multiple date/time options from property owner
Reach out to photographer network to find earliest availability
Confirm appointment once both parties agree on timing
photographer_availability_scheduling.suggested_response: Thank you for providing your availability. We've reached out to our photographer to find their soonest availability and will circle back with you to confirm a time and day that works for both parties.

lockbox_installation_confirmation.answer: Photographers/vendors confirm lockbox installations by providing lockbox numbers, codes, locations, and key inventory details after completing the setup.
talking_points: Smart Lockbox (SLB) number and physical location on property
Classic Lockbox (CLB) code and placement details
Key inventory confirmation (which keys are in which lockbox)
lockbox_installation_confirmation.suggested_response: Thank you for the lockbox installation update. We have recorded the SLB/CLB details and updated the property dashboard accordingly.

questionnaire_completion_follow_up.answer: After completing the onboarding questionnaire, the next step is scheduling the listing appointment for photography, inspection, and lockbox placement.
talking_points: Listing appointment includes professional photography
Initial property walkthrough/inspection conducted
Smart and classic lockboxes placed with key organization
questionnaire_completion_follow_up.suggested_response: Thank you for completing the questionnaire! The next step is to schedule the Listing Appointment. We've reached out to the photographer to find their soonest availability and will circle back with you to confirm a time and day.

missing_questionnaire_information.answer: The onboarding team requests specific missing information such as utility provider details, breaker panel location, and water shutoff location to complete property records.
talking_points: Breaker panel and water shutoff locations for emergency access
Utility provider information (electricity, gas, water, garbage)
Essential for property management and tenant communications
missing_questionnaire_information.suggested_response: Thank you for completing the onboarding questionnaire! We noticed there are a few missing details. Could you please provide the breaker panel location, water shutoff location, and utility provider information at your earliest convenience?

listing_price_recommendation.answer: Ziprent provides market analysis-based pricing recommendations, with owners having final approval on listing price and the ability to adjust based on market performance.
talking_points: Market analysis considers comparable properties and square footage
Owners can accept, reject, or request price adjustments
Price can be modified if listing underperforms
listing_price_recommendation.suggested_response: Based on our market analysis, we recommend a listing price of $X,XXX per month. This takes into account comparable properties in your area and current market conditions. Please let us know if you'd like to proceed with this price or if you'd prefer a different amount.

hoa_documentation_request.answer: HOA documents (CC&Rs, Rules & Regulations, contact information) are required for properties with homeowner associations to ensure compliance and efficient tenant management.
talking_points: CC&Rs, Rules & Regulations, and fee schedules needed
HOA contact information and email for direct communication
Required addendums may be added to lease based on HOA rules
hoa_documentation_request.suggested_response: We're reaching out to request HOA documents including CC&Rs, Rules & Regulations, and any other documents pertaining to community rules, processes, or fees. Please also provide HOA contact information so we can communicate efficiently during our management.

listing_live_confirmation.answer: Once a listing goes live, it's syndicated across Zillow, RentPath, and Zumper networks, with some platforms updating instantly and others within a few hours.
talking_points: Listing appears on Zillow, HotPads, Trulia instantly
Other syndication partners may take a few hours to update
Craigslist and Apartments.com no longer used due to platform limitations
listing_live_confirmation.suggested_response: Your listing is live! Your property at [address] at $X,XXX/month is now live across the Zillow, RentPath and Zumper networks. While Zillow, HotPads, and Trulia should show the property instantly, the info may take a few hours to update across all platforms.

insurance_certificate_requirements.answer: Ziprent requires property insurance certificates with general liability coverage of at least a specific amount and Ziprent listed as additional interested party.
talking_points: General liability coverage must meet minimum requirements
Ziprent must be listed as additional interested party
Certificate reviewed to ensure compliance before listing
insurance_certificate_requirements.suggested_response: Thank you for sending your insurance certificate. Upon review, we need to ensure it meets Ziprent's requirements: general liability coverage in the required amount and Ziprent listed as additional interested party. Please contact your insurance provider to update the certificate accordingly.

photographer_appointment_cancellation_re.answer: Photographers can cancel appointments due to emergencies or conflicts, and the onboarding team reassigns to another photographer or reschedules based on new availability.
talking_points: Notify onboarding team immediately if unable to attend
Team will reassign to another available photographer
Coordinate new appointment time with owner and photographer
photographer_appointment_cancellation_re.suggested_response: Thank you for notifying us about the cancellation. We will reassign this appointment to another photographer and coordinate with the owner to find a new time that works for everyone.

showing_availability_schedule.answer: Owners provide specific days and time windows for property showings, which are set up as recurring weekly schedules or open house appointments.
talking_points: Collect preferred showing days and time windows from owner
Can be set as rotating weekly schedule or specific dates
Owners can modify showing schedule anytime by contacting team
showing_availability_schedule.suggested_response: Thank you for providing your showing availability. We've added your preferred times to the system and set this schedule to rotate weekly. Please let us know if you'd like to make any changes to these times.

property_management_agreement_signature.answer: Property Management Agreement must be signed before proceeding with listing process; owners receive digital agreement via portal for e-signature.
talking_points: Agreement sent electronically through owner portal
All listed owners/signatories must sign before proceeding
Signatories can be added or removed upon request
property_management_agreement_signature.suggested_response: We noticed you haven't signed the property management agreement yet. Please log into your account and sign the agreement so we can proceed with listing your property. Once signed, we'll move forward with the next steps.

lockbox_pickup_coordination.answer: Lockbox pickups are coordinated when tenants move in or properties are taken off market, with photographers/vendors confirming pickup and key retrieval.
talking_points: Pickup scheduled when property is leased or removed from market
Vendor confirms if keys remain in lockbox for tenant access
CLB may be left for tenant use; SLB typically retrieved
lockbox_pickup_coordination.suggested_response: We have scheduled lockbox pickup at [address]. Please confirm when you've completed the pickup and let us know the status of any keys that were in the lockbox.

property_not_ready_rescheduling.answer: When properties are not ready for photography (cleaning, repairs, staging incomplete), appointments are rescheduled for a later date when work is finished.
talking_points: Owner should notify team if property needs additional work
Appointments rescheduled once property is photo-ready
Communicate expected completion date for proper scheduling
property_not_ready_rescheduling.suggested_response: Thank you for letting us know the property isn't ready yet. We'll reschedule the listing appointment for [new date]. Please reach out once the property is completely ready and we'll coordinate with our photographer.

listing_corrections_updates.answer: Owners can request listing corrections for inaccurate information, photos, pricing, or availability dates, which the team updates promptly.
talking_points: Review listing details and request specific changes needed
Team updates listing across all syndication platforms
Changes typically reflected within hours of request
listing_corrections_updates.suggested_response: Thank you for bringing this to our attention. We've made the corrections to your listing as requested. The updates should be reflected across all platforms shortly. Please let us know if you need any other changes.

owner_provided_photos_usage.answer: Owner-provided photos can be used if they meet Ziprent's professional standards and photo requirements; permission and confirmation of ownership must be obtained.
talking_points: Photos must meet quality and professional standards
Owner must confirm they own rights to photos
Written permission required for third-party photographer images
owner_provided_photos_usage.suggested_response: Thank you for offering to provide existing photos. We'll need your confirmation that you own the rights to these photos and permission to use them. Please note they must meet our photo standards; if not, we'll proceed with our photographer during the listing appointment.

how_do_i_know_if_repair_is_owner_or_tena.answer: Refer to the lease agreement for specific responsibilities. Common tenant responsibilities include filters, batteries, bulbs, pest control (ants, roaches, spiders), and routine maintenance items.
talking_points: Lease agreement outlines specific owner vs tenant responsibilities
Tenants typically responsible for: HVAC filters, light bulbs, batteries, smoke detector maintenance, and common household pests (ants, roaches, spiders, small bee nests, rodents)
Owners typically responsible for: structural repairs, appliance repairs/replacements, habitability issues, and major system failures
Gray areas should be reviewed case-by-case with reference to lease terms
how_do_i_know_if_repair_is_owner_or_tena.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. According to your lease agreement, [specific item] is the [owner's/tenant's] responsibility. As outlined in the lease, tenants are responsible for maintenance items like filters, light bulbs, batteries, and common household pests, while owners handle structural repairs and major systems. If you need clarification on a specific item, please let us know.

can_i_use_my_own_vendor_for_repairs.answer: Yes, owners can use their own vendors. They need to provide vendor contact information and either coordinate scheduling directly or have Ziprent assist with tenant coordination.
talking_points: Owners can use their own vendors by clicking 'Use My Own Vendor' button or providing contact details
Vendor must meet requirements: English-speaking, responsive (6-hour email response for urgent), able to schedule with tenants, accept payment terms
Owner can either coordinate directly with vendor and tenant, or Ziprent can assist with scheduling coordination
Once work is complete, owner should confirm completion so ticket can be closed
can_i_use_my_own_vendor_for_repairs.suggested_response: Thank you for letting us know you'd like to use your own vendor. Please provide their contact information (name, phone, email) and we'll update the ticket accordingly. You can coordinate scheduling directly with them and the tenant, or we're happy to assist. Once the work is completed, please let us know so we can close the ticket.

what_happens_if_owner_doesnt_respond_to_.answer: If no response within 72 hours, Ziprent will automatically approve the most readily available vendor for habitability concerns within threshold limits set in the Property Management Agreement.
talking_points: 72-hour response window for bid approvals
For habitability concerns: Ziprent auto-approves most readily available vendor within agreement threshold
For non-habitability concerns: different protocol may apply
This ensures urgent repairs aren't delayed while protecting tenant welfare
what_happens_if_owner_doesnt_respond_to_.suggested_response: This is a friendly reminder that we need your approval to proceed with the repair. If we don't hear back within 72 hours, and this is a habitability concern, Ziprent will automatically approve the most readily available vendor within the threshold set in our Property Management Agreement to ensure the issue is addressed promptly.

how_do_i_troubleshoot_before_sending_ven.answer: Common troubleshooting includes checking breakers/GFCI outlets, replacing batteries, resetting appliances by unplugging, checking filters, and verifying simple fixes like switches or remote controls.
talking_points: Electrical issues: check breaker panel, reset tripped breakers, check GFCI outlets
Appliances: try unplugging and replugging, check power source, verify breaker isn't tripped
Disposal: check reset button underneath unit, verify breaker is on
Thermostats/remotes: replace batteries first
These simple steps can often resolve issues without vendor visit
how_do_i_troubleshoot_before_sending_ven.suggested_response: Before we send a vendor, could you try [specific troubleshooting step]? Often this resolves the issue without needing a service call. Please let us know if this works, and if not, we'll proceed with scheduling a vendor visit.

what_is_vendor_bidding_process_timeline.answer: Ziprent collects bids from vendor network, sends them to owner for approval, then schedules once approved. Timeline varies but urgent/time-critical repairs are prioritized.
talking_points: Process: Review → Bidding (collecting bids) → Approving (owner review) → Scheduling → Confirmed → Complete
Multiple vendors contacted simultaneously for faster turnaround
Time-critical repairs (move-in deadlines) are marked as priority
Owners receive bids with recommendations based on price, experience, and reviews
what_is_vendor_bidding_process_timeline.suggested_response: We are currently in the process of collecting bids from our vendor network. No action is needed from you at this moment. We'll send you the bids for approval as soon as we receive them, and we're working as diligently as possible to get this scheduled quickly.

how_does_warranty_work_for_previous_repa.answer: If same issue recurs shortly after repair, contact the original vendor about warranty coverage. Ziprent will reach out to vendor to request warranty service at no additional charge.
talking_points: Most vendors provide warranty periods on their work (typically 30-90 days)
If same issue returns, original vendor should be contacted first for warranty service
Ziprent will coordinate warranty visits and ensure no additional charges for covered work
Vendors typically need to verify issue is same/related to original repair
how_does_warranty_work_for_previous_repa.suggested_response: Thank you for reporting this. Since this appears to be the same issue that was recently repaired, we'll reach out to the original vendor to request a warranty visit. Most vendors warranty their work, so there should be no additional charge if this is related to the previous repair. We'll keep you updated.

can_tenant_be_reimbursed_for_repairs_the.answer: Tenants can request reimbursement for emergency repairs or owner-approved expenses. Owner must approve the reimbursement amount, and tenant should provide receipts.
talking_points: Emergency repairs: tenant may need to handle immediately and seek reimbursement
Owner approval required for reimbursement amount
Tenant must provide receipts/invoices documenting expense
Unauthorized repairs may not be reimbursed
can_tenant_be_reimbursed_for_repairs_the.suggested_response: We'll check with the owner regarding reimbursement for the repair expense you incurred. Please provide us with the receipt/invoice showing the amount paid. Once we receive owner approval, we can process the reimbursement of $[amount].

what_if_vendor_cannot_complete_job_or_de.answer: If vendor declines or cannot complete job, Ziprent thanks them and reaches out to alternative vendors from the network to gather new bids.
talking_points: Vendors may decline due to: location not serviced, lack of expertise, unavailable timeframe, or scope beyond capability
Ziprent maintains large vendor network for backup options
Process moves to next available qualified vendor
No penalty to vendor for declining work outside their scope
what_if_vendor_cannot_complete_job_or_de.suggested_response: Thank you for letting us know. We understand this job isn't a good fit for your services. We'll reach out to other vendors in our network and keep you in mind for future opportunities.

how_do_i_schedule_appointment_with_vendo.answer: Tenants should call vendor directly using contact info provided in approval email, then confirm appointment time with Ziprent. Vendors use 'Confirm Appointment' button to document scheduled time.
talking_points: Approved vendor email includes full contact details for tenant to call
'Confirm Appointment' button is for documenting agreed time, not for automatic scheduling
Tenant and vendor coordinate directly on convenient time/date
Once appointment is set, vendor (or tenant) should notify Ziprent of confirmed time using provided button
how_do_i_schedule_appointment_with_vendo.suggested_response: The following job has been approved. Please call the vendor at [phone number] to schedule an appointment at a time that works for you. Once you've confirmed a date and time with them, please let us know or use the 'Confirm Appointment' button so we can update our system.

what_information_do_i_need_to_provide_fo.answer: Provide detailed description of issue, what troubleshooting was attempted, photos/videos when possible, and preferred scheduling availability. More details help vendors provide accurate bids.
talking_points: Clear description: what's not working, when it started, what symptoms are present
Troubleshooting already done: what was tried that didn't work
Photos and videos extremely helpful for accurate diagnosis and quotes
Scheduling availability: days/times tenant is available for vendor visit
what_information_do_i_need_to_provide_fo.suggested_response: Thank you for submitting this repair request. To help us proceed, could you please provide [more details/photos/clarification] about the issue? Specifically, we'd like to know [specific questions]. This information will help our vendors provide accurate estimates and resolve the issue efficiently.

what_if_issue_is_hoa_responsibility.answer: HOA-related issues (common areas, building systems, exterior maintenance in some properties) should be reported to HOA directly. Owner or property manager coordinates with HOA.
talking_points: HOA typically handles: common area repairs, building exterior, shared fences, landscaping (in some communities), main building doors/locks
Owner needs to contact HOA management to initiate repairs
Ziprent can facilitate communication but cannot directly manage HOA vendors
Tenants may need to be patient as HOA repairs follow different approval processes
what_if_issue_is_hoa_responsibility.suggested_response: Thank you for reporting this. This issue is actually the HOA's responsibility to address. We'll reach out to the HOA on your behalf [or: The owner will need to contact the HOA directly to request this repair]. HOA repairs can take additional time due to their approval processes, but we'll keep you updated on the status.

how_does_home_warranty_process_work.answer: Property owners must initiate home warranty claims directly with their warranty company. Ziprent does not hold the policy and cannot file claims, but can assist with vendor coordination once claim is opened.
talking_points: Owner initiates and manages all home warranty claims directly
Ziprent cannot file warranty claims as we don't hold the policy
Owner pays service fee directly to warranty company
If owner wants to use Ziprent network instead, they can opt out of warranty for that repair
Warranty repairs may take longer due to warranty company approval processes
how_does_home_warranty_process_work.suggested_response: Since you have a home warranty on this property, you'll need to initiate the claim directly with your warranty provider. Ziprent doesn't hold the policy, so we're unable to file claims on your behalf. If you'd prefer to use our vendor network instead for faster service, we're happy to proceed with collecting bids. Please let us know how you'd like to proceed.

can_repair_be_combined_with_other_open_t.answer: Yes, multiple related repairs can often be combined into one vendor visit to save time and money. Ziprent will coordinate with vendor to address multiple items in single appointment.
talking_points: Combining repairs saves on multiple service call fees
Works best when repairs are similar trade (all plumbing, all electrical, all handyman work)
Vendor provides comprehensive quote covering all items
More efficient for tenant (one appointment instead of multiple)
can_repair_be_combined_with_other_open_t.suggested_response: We notice there are multiple repair requests at your property. We can have the same vendor address all of these items in one visit, which would be more efficient and cost-effective. We'll reach out to vendors who can handle all of these repairs together and send you a combined quote.

what_are_time_critical_or_urgent_repair_.answer: Time-critical repairs have deadlines (often tenant move-in dates). Urgent repairs affect habitability or safety. These receive priority processing and faster vendor approval to meet deadlines.
talking_points: TIME CRITICAL: repairs with specific deadline (usually move-in date), marked in subject line
URGENT: habitability/safety issues (no heat, no water, major leaks, electrical hazards)
Priority processing: faster bid collection, owner auto-approval if no response within 72 hours
Move-in related repairs must be completed before tenant arrival
what_are_time_critical_or_urgent_repair_.suggested_response: This is marked as a TIME CRITICAL repair with a completion deadline of [date] due to the upcoming tenant move-in. We're prioritizing this and working to get bids to you as quickly as possible. Please review and approve at your earliest convenience so we can meet this deadline.

how_do_i_close_completed_repair_ticket.answer: Vendor uploads invoice and photos, then tenant or owner confirms work was completed satisfactorily. Use 'Mark as Complete' button to close ticket in system.
talking_points: Vendor should upload invoice and completion photos after visit
Tenant/owner confirms work completed to satisfaction
'Mark as Complete' button officially closes ticket
If issue persists, don't mark complete and notify Ziprent immediately
how_do_i_close_completed_repair_ticket.suggested_response: The vendor has informed us that the work has been completed. We'd like to check in with you to confirm whether everything was completed to your satisfaction so we can proceed with closing out this work order. Please use the 'Mark as Complete' button if everything looks good, or let us know if there are any concerns.

what_if_tenant_is_not_available_for_sche.answer: Tenant must provide scheduling availability and be available during confirmed appointment time. Lockbox codes provided to approved vendors for access when arrangements are made.
talking_points: Tenant provides availability windows when submitting repair request
Vendor coordinates with tenant to find mutually convenient time
Lockbox codes shared with approved vendors after scheduling confirmed
If tenant unavailable, reschedule to avoid vendor trip charges
what_if_tenant_is_not_available_for_sche.suggested_response: We have a vendor ready to schedule this repair. Please let us know your availability (days and time windows) so we can coordinate the appointment. Once a time is confirmed with the vendor, we'll provide them with access information.

can_tenant_install_or_modify_things_at_p.answer: Tenants need owner approval before installing, mounting, or modifying property features (even if they pay for it). This includes fixtures, appliances, locks, and anything requiring drilling or permanent attachment.
talking_points: Owner approval required for ANY installations or modifications
This includes: mounting TVs, installing fixtures, adding locks, changing hardware, permanent attachments
Even if tenant pays, permission still required
Unauthorized modifications may result in charges at move-out
can_tenant_install_or_modify_things_at_p.suggested_response: Thank you for your request. Any installations or modifications to the property require owner approval, even if you're willing to pay for them. We'll reach out to the owner and let you know their decision. Please don't proceed with any changes until we receive their approval.

what_if_property_has_shared_fence_or_cos.answer: Shared fences between properties typically have shared repair costs with neighbors. Owner should coordinate with neighbor/neighbor's owner to split costs before proceeding.
talking_points: Shared fences are typically shared cost responsibility between adjacent property owners
Owner should contact neighbor to discuss repair and cost-sharing
Get neighbor contact information and agreement before proceeding
Some jurisdictions have specific laws about shared fence responsibilities
what_if_property_has_shared_fence_or_cos.suggested_response: Since this is a shared fence with the neighboring property, repair costs are typically split between both property owners.
