Could you reach out to the neighbor or the neighboring property owner to discuss coordinating this repair? Once you have their agreement on cost-sharing, please let us know so we can proceed with gathering bids.

how_do_minimum_visit_fees_and_nte_work.answer: Minimum visit fee is charged for vendor to assess. Not-to-exceed (NTE) is max cost without additional approval. Repairs under NTE proceed; over NTE requires owner approval for additional costs.
talking_points: Minimum visit fee: diagnostic/assessment charge (e.g., $85, $120)
Not-to-exceed (NTE): vendor can complete repairs up to this amount without re-approval
If repair exceeds NTE, vendor must provide estimate and get owner approval before continuing
This system allows quick resolution of minor issues while protecting against unexpected major costs
how_do_minimum_visit_fees_and_nte_work.suggested_response: This vendor charges a $[X] minimum visit fee to assess the issue. They're authorized to complete repairs up to $[NTE amount] without additional approval. If the repair will cost more than that, they'll provide an estimate and we'll get your approval before proceeding with any additional work.

what_if_owner_wants_to_do_repair_themsel.answer: Owners can perform repairs themselves if they prefer. They should coordinate access with tenant, complete repairs in reasonable timeframe, and notify Ziprent when work is done so ticket can be closed.
talking_points: Owner must coordinate access time directly with tenant
Should complete repairs within reasonable timeframe, especially for habitability issues
Keep tenant informed of timeline and any delays
Notify Ziprent when work complete so ticket can be closed
what_if_owner_wants_to_do_repair_themsel.suggested_response: Thank you for letting us know you'll handle this repair yourself. Please coordinate directly with the tenant regarding access and timing. Once you've completed the work, please let us know so we can close the ticket. If you need the tenant's contact information, we're happy to provide it.

what_if_issue_was_not_present_at_move_in.answer: For issues not documented at move-in that appear to be damage vs. wear-and-tear, tenant may be responsible. Owner decides on case-by-case basis. Move-in photos and condition reports are evidence.
talking_points: Move-in photos and condition reports document pre-existing conditions
Tenant responsible for damage they cause beyond normal wear and tear
Owner makes determination based on: move-in documentation, nature of issue, timeline
If tenant-caused damage, tenant should hire and pay vendor directly
what_if_issue_was_not_present_at_move_in.suggested_response: Thank you for reporting this. Based on the move-in documentation, this doesn't appear to be a pre-existing condition. The owner has determined this is [tenant responsibility/will be addressed as owner expense]. [If tenant responsibility:] You'll need to arrange and pay for this repair directly. If you need vendor recommendations, we're happy to provide them.

how_do_i_get_lockbox_code_or_property_ac.answer: Lockbox codes are provided to approved vendors after appointment is scheduled. Tenants receive access information at move-in. Codes are security-sensitive and only shared on need-to-know basis.
talking_points: Vendors receive lockbox codes only after they're approved and appointment is scheduled
This protects property security
Tenants receive all access information (keys, codes, garage openers) at move-in
Lost or missing access items should be reported immediately
how_do_i_get_lockbox_code_or_property_ac.suggested_response: Once we confirm your appointment for [date/time], we'll provide you with the access codes you need. For security reasons, we only share this information with approved vendors after scheduling is confirmed.

what_if_vendor_needs_follow_up_visit_or_.answer: If vendor determines additional work needed beyond original scope, they provide estimate for additional work and owner must approve before proceeding.
talking_points: Initial diagnosis may reveal additional issues
Vendor provides detailed estimate for additional work needed
Owner approval required before proceeding with work outside original scope
Keeps owners informed and in control of repair costs
what_if_vendor_needs_follow_up_visit_or_.suggested_response: The vendor has completed their initial assessment and determined that additional work is needed beyond the original repair. They've provided an estimate of $[amount] for [description of additional work]. Please review and let us know if you'd like to approve this additional work so the vendor can proceed.

what_if_multiple_vendors_have_contacted_.answer: Ziprent contacts multiple vendors simultaneously to gather competitive bids quickly. Owner reviews all bids and selects preferred vendor. Non-selected vendors are notified they weren't chosen.
talking_points: Multiple vendors contacted at once for faster bid collection
Provides owner with competitive pricing options
Owner selects based on price, reviews, availability, and previous experience
Non-selected vendors thanked and told they weren't chosen this time
what_if_multiple_vendors_have_contacted_.suggested_response: We contact multiple vendors simultaneously to gather competitive bids and ensure we can meet any timeline requirements. You may hear from several vendors, but we'll send you all the bids together so you can review and select your preferred option. We appreciate your patience as we collect these estimates.

how_to_provide_vendor_contact_informatio.answer: Owners can provide their preferred vendor's contact information directly via email, and Ziprent will add them to the system and coordinate scheduling.
talking_points: Owners can reply with vendor name and phone number
Ziprent will update the system to reflect 'Owner Vendor'
Tenant will be instructed to contact the vendor directly or Ziprent will coordinate
Owner is responsible for payment to their own vendor
how_to_provide_vendor_contact_informatio.suggested_response: Thank you for providing your vendor's contact information. We've updated the system to reflect [Vendor Name] as your preferred vendor for this job. We'll coordinate with the tenant on scheduling and keep you updated on progress.

how_to_update_appointment_schedule.answer: Vendors should confirm appointment dates and times using the 'Confirm Appointment' button or by replying via email with the scheduled date/time.
talking_points: Use the 'Confirm Appointment' button in the email
Alternatively, reply with confirmed date and time
Ziprent will update system notes and notify all parties
Lockbox code provided once appointment is confirmed
how_to_update_appointment_schedule.suggested_response: Thank you for confirming the appointment. We've updated our system to reflect [date] at [time]. We'll notify the tenant and provide you with access details. Please keep us posted on how the visit goes.

how_to_submit_invoice_after_completion.answer: Vendors should attach the invoice and job completion photos by replying to the repair email thread or using the 'Upload Invoice' button.
talking_points: Reply to the email with invoice and photos attached
Use 'Upload Invoice' button if available
Include job completion photos for tenant confirmation
Payment processed within 3 business days after verification
how_to_submit_invoice_after_completion.suggested_response: Thank you for completing the job. Please send us the invoice and job completion pictures by attaching them to this email thread. Once we receive them and confirm with the tenant, we'll process your payment within three business days.

how_to_receive_payment_as_vendor.answer: Ziprent pays vendors via direct deposit (ACH). Vendors need to provide bank account number, routing number, bank name, and account holder name.
talking_points: Direct deposit is the only payment method
Provide: account number, routing number, account type, bank name, account holder name
Payment processed within 3 business days after invoice submission
Setup only needed once, then stored in system
how_to_receive_payment_as_vendor.suggested_response: We make payments via direct deposit only. To receive payment within three business days, please provide: 1) Account number, 2) Routing number, 3) Account type, 4) Bank's name, and 5) Name on the account. This information will be securely stored for future jobs.

what_if_vendor_unavailable_or_declines.answer: When a vendor is unavailable or declines a job, Ziprent will exclude them from that specific job and find an alternative vendor.
talking_points: Thank the vendor for letting Ziprent know
Vendor will be excluded from this specific job only
Will be considered for future jobs in their service area
Alternative vendors will be sourced immediately
what_if_vendor_unavailable_or_declines.suggested_response: Thank you for letting us know. We'll exclude you from this job and reach out to other available vendors. We appreciate your response and look forward to working with you on future jobs.

how_owner_approves_repair_bids.answer: Owners receive bids via email and should use the 'Approve' button or reply with their selection. If no response in 72 hours, Ziprent may auto-approve habitability concerns.
talking_points: Bids are sent via email with vendor details and pricing
Use 'Approve' button or reply with vendor choice
72-hour response window before auto-approval (habitability issues)
Can request additional bids if needed
how_owner_approves_repair_bids.suggested_response: We've gathered bids for your review. Please use the 'Approve' button to select your preferred vendor. If we don't hear back within 72 hours, we may auto-approve the most readily available vendor for habitability concerns per your Property Management Agreement.

how_to_request_additional_bids.answer: Owners can reply to the bid email requesting more vendor options, and Ziprent will reach out to additional vendors in the network.
talking_points: Reply to the bid email with request for more options
Specify if looking for different price range or specialty
Ziprent will source 2-3 additional vendors
May take 24-48 hours depending on vendor availability
how_to_request_additional_bids.suggested_response: Thank you for your feedback. We'll reach out to additional vendors and share their bids with you for comparison. We'll get back to you as soon as we receive their responses.

what_if_vendor_sends_follow_up_estimate.answer: If a vendor needs to provide an additional estimate after inspection, they should use the 'Upload Estimate' button or reply to the email with the revised quote.
talking_points: Use 'Upload Estimate' button in email
Or reply to email thread with updated pricing
Include detailed breakdown if cost exceeds original estimate
Owner approval required before proceeding with additional work
what_if_vendor_sends_follow_up_estimate.suggested_response: Thank you for the updated estimate. We'll forward this to the owner for review and approval. We'll let you know as soon as we receive their decision.

how_to_handle_warranty_or_follow_up_visi.answer: For warranty issues or incomplete repairs, Ziprent contacts the original vendor to schedule a follow-up visit at no additional charge.
talking_points: Original vendor contacted for warranty work
No additional charge for warranty repairs
New ticket may be created but linked to original job
If vendor unavailable, alternative vendor may be assigned
how_to_handle_warranty_or_follow_up_visi.suggested_response: Thank you for reporting this issue. We'll contact the original vendor to schedule a warranty visit. We'll keep you posted on their availability and next steps.

how_to_access_property_with_lockbox.answer: Vendors receive lockbox codes via email after confirming their appointment date and time. Access details include lockbox location and entry instructions.
talking_points: Lockbox code sent after appointment confirmation
May require building call box or tenant coordination
Smart lockbox codes are time-sensitive
Contact Ziprent if access issues arise
how_to_access_property_with_lockbox.suggested_response: Thank you for confirming the appointment. Here are the access details: [lockbox type and location]. The code is [code]. Please ensure the property is secured after your visit.

what_if_owner_wants_to_use_own_vendor.answer: Owners can use their own vendors by providing contact information. The owner is responsible for coordinating payment directly with their vendor.
talking_points: Provide vendor name and contact number to Ziprent
Ziprent marks job as 'Owner Vendor' in system
Owner responsible for direct payment to their vendor
Ziprent coordinates scheduling and updates with tenant
what_if_owner_wants_to_use_own_vendor.suggested_response: Thank you for providing your vendor's information. We've updated the ticket to reflect that you're using your own vendor. Please note that you'll be responsible for coordinating payment directly with them. We'll handle scheduling and keep you updated.

how_to_cancel_or_close_repair_ticket.answer: Repair tickets are closed when the owner declines the repair, the issue is resolved by owner/tenant, or the job is completed and confirmed.
talking_points: Owner can decline repair via email
Ticket closed if owner/tenant resolves independently
Automatically closed after invoice processing
Confirmation required before closure
how_to_cancel_or_close_repair_ticket.suggested_response: Thank you for the update. We'll go ahead and close this ticket as requested. If you need any further assistance, please don't hesitate to reach out.

what_if_repair_estimate_exceeds_budget.answer: When estimates exceed the not-to-exceed amount, vendors must get owner approval before proceeding. Detailed proposals are sent for amounts over agreed thresholds.
talking_points: Stop work if estimate exceeds not-to-exceed amount
Submit detailed proposal to owner via Ziprent
Owner approval required before continuing
Minimum visit fee typically still applies
what_if_repair_estimate_exceeds_budget.suggested_response: The repair cost is estimated to exceed the approved amount. We've sent a detailed proposal to the owner for review. We'll let you know once we receive their approval to proceed.

how_to_handle_urgent_or_time_critical_re.answer: Urgent repairs are marked with [URGENT] or [TIME CRITICAL] tags. Ziprent prioritizes fastest available vendors and may auto-approve within 72 hours for habitability issues.
talking_points: Urgent repairs prioritized for same-day or next-day service
Fastest available vendor typically selected
Auto-approval possible after 72 hours for habitability concerns
Move-in deadlines clearly communicated to vendors
how_to_handle_urgent_or_time_critical_re.suggested_response: This is marked as an urgent repair. We're reaching out to vendors who can respond quickly. What is your soonest availability for this job if approved?

what_if_tenant_not_responding_to_vendor.answer: If tenants don't respond to vendor scheduling attempts, Ziprent follows up directly with the tenant and coordinates scheduling on their behalf.
talking_points: Vendor should notify Ziprent of no response
Ziprent contacts tenant via phone and email
Alternative appointment times arranged
Lockbox access provided if tenant unavailable
what_if_tenant_not_responding_to_vendor.suggested_response: Thank you for letting us know. We'll follow up with the tenant directly to coordinate scheduling and get back to you with confirmed availability shortly.

how_to_combine_multiple_repair_requests.answer: Multiple related repairs can be combined into one job. Owners or tenants should request consolidation, and Ziprent will merge tickets to avoid multiple service fees.
talking_points: Request ticket consolidation via email
Prevents multiple vendor visit fees
All issues addressed in single appointment
Original tickets closed and merged into primary ticket
how_to_combine_multiple_repair_requests.suggested_response: We'll merge these related repair requests into one job as you requested. This will help avoid multiple service call fees. We'll close out the duplicate ticket and update you on the combined job.

what_if_vendor_needs_parts_ordered.answer: Vendors should inform Ziprent when parts need to be ordered, provide estimated arrival time, and schedule follow-up visit once parts arrive.
talking_points: Notify Ziprent of parts needed and ETA
Original diagnostic fee typically applies
Follow-up visit scheduled after parts arrival
Keep all parties updated on timeline
what_if_vendor_needs_parts_ordered.suggested_response: Thank you for the update. Please keep us informed when the parts arrive, and we'll coordinate the follow-up visit with the tenant. We'll update the owner on the timeline.

how_to_request_photos_or_more_informatio.answer: Ziprent can request additional photos or details from tenants/owners to help vendors provide accurate estimates before visiting the property.
talking_points: Request photos via email to get better assessment
Helps vendors provide more accurate estimates
Reduces need for diagnostic visits when possible
Photos added to work order for vendor reference
how_to_request_photos_or_more_informatio.suggested_response: To help provide an accurate estimate, could you please share photos of [specific issue]? This will help us and the vendor better assess the repair needs.

what_is_minimum_visit_fee_policy.answer: Most vendors charge a minimum visit/diagnostic fee (typically $60-$150) which often applies toward the repair cost if the job is completed.
talking_points: Diagnostic fee covers inspection and assessment
Usually credited toward repair if job completed same day
Typical range: $60-$150 depending on trade and location
Disclosed in bid before owner approval
what_is_minimum_visit_fee_policy.suggested_response: The vendor's minimum visit fee is $[amount], which will be credited toward the repair if completed for less than $[not-to-exceed amount]. If the repair exceeds that amount, they'll provide an updated estimate for your approval.

how_to_handle_home_warranty_repairs.answer: For home warranty repairs, the owner coordinates with their warranty company, and Ziprent facilitates scheduling between the warranty vendor and tenant.
talking_points: Owner must file claim with warranty company
Warranty company assigns their approved vendor
Ziprent coordinates scheduling only
Owner responsible for any warranty deductibles
how_to_handle_home_warranty_repairs.suggested_response: We understand you're using your home warranty for this repair. Please let us know once the warranty company provides scheduling details, and we'll coordinate with the tenant to ensure access.

what_if_issue_is_tenant_responsibility.answer: If an issue is determined to be tenant responsibility (per lease), Ziprent notifies the tenant and may still coordinate repair with tenant covering the cost.
talking_points: Refer to lease terms for responsibility determination
Common tenant items: batteries, light bulbs, pest control, filters
Tenant can hire own service or pay for Ziprent-coordinated repair
Owner may choose to cover as courtesy
what_if_issue_is_tenant_responsibility.suggested_response: Per your lease agreement, [specific issue] is tenant responsibility. However, if you'd like to proceed with a repair through our vendor, the cost will be deducted from your security deposit or invoiced to you directly.

how_to_handle_move_in_or_move_out_repair.answer: Time-critical move-in repairs are prioritized with tight deadlines. Move-out repairs may be charged to outgoing tenant's security deposit if damage exceeds normal wear.
talking_points: Move-in repairs completed before tenant arrival when possible
Deadlines clearly communicated in subject line
Move-out repairs assessed for tenant vs. owner responsibility
Detailed photos and invoices required for deposit deductions
how_to_handle_move_in_or_move_out_repair.suggested_response: This is a time-critical repair that must be completed by [date] for tenant move-in. Please confirm your soonest availability to meet this deadline.

what_if_owner_wants_to_handle_repair_the.answer: Owners can choose to handle repairs themselves. They should notify Ziprent to close the ticket and inform the tenant directly of their plan.
talking_points: Owner notifies Ziprent they'll handle personally
Ziprent closes ticket and updates tenant
Owner responsible for scheduling and access with tenant
Owner should share completion confirmation with Ziprent
what_if_owner_wants_to_handle_repair_the.suggested_response: Thank you for letting us know you'll handle this repair personally. We'll close this ticket and notify the tenant. Please keep us updated once the repair is complete.

how_to_handle_vendor_no_show.answer: If a vendor misses a scheduled appointment without notice, Ziprent follows up with the vendor and may reassign to a more reliable alternative.
talking_points: Tenant or owner reports no-show to Ziprent
Ziprent attempts to contact vendor immediately
Alternative vendor sourced if original unresponsive
Repeated no-shows result in vendor removal from network
how_to_handle_vendor_no_show.suggested_response: We apologize for the inconvenience. We're reaching out to the vendor to find out what happened. In the meantime, we're sourcing an alternative vendor to ensure this gets resolved quickly.

how_to_troubleshoot_before_vendor_visit.answer: Ziprent may ask tenants to try simple troubleshooting steps (reset breakers, check batteries, clear drains) before dispatching a vendor to save costs.
talking_points: Simple fixes attempted first (breakers, filters, batteries)
Helps avoid unnecessary service calls
Detailed troubleshooting questions asked upfront
Vendor dispatched if troubleshooting unsuccessful
how_to_troubleshoot_before_vendor_visit.suggested_response: Before we dispatch a vendor, could you please try [specific troubleshooting step]? This may resolve the issue quickly. Please let us know the result, and we'll proceed accordingly.

what_if_repair_requires_specialized_vend.answer: Some repairs require specialized vendors (e.g., manufacturer-approved appliance repair, licensed specialists). Ziprent sources appropriate vendors for the trade.
talking_points: Certain repairs need specialized licensing or certification
Manufacturer warranties may require approved vendors
Specialized vendors may have limited availability
Higher costs typical for specialized trades
what_if_repair_requires_specialized_vend.suggested_response: This repair requires a specialized vendor. We're reaching out to qualified professionals in our network and will share their bids with you shortly.

when_will_i_receive_payment.answer: Payments are processed via ACH direct deposit and typically appear in vendor accounts within 3-5 business days after invoice approval and processing.
talking_points: Payment method is ACH direct deposit only
Allow 3-5 business days for funds to appear after processing
Confirm payment has been initiated and provide processing date if available
when_will_i_receive_payment.suggested_response: Hello, this has already been paid. Please allow 3-5 business days for the funds to appear in your account. If you need further details about the payment, please let us know.

what_is_your_earliest_availability.answer: When collecting bids, Ziprent asks vendors for their soonest availability to visit the property and perform the work.
talking_points: Request vendor's earliest availability window
Confirm scheduling constraints (time critical repairs, tenant move-ins)
Recap availability details in writing for confirmation
what_is_your_earliest_availability.suggested_response: Thank you for speaking with me. Just to recap our call: you informed us that you can visit the property [insert date/time window]. We will update our system and keep you posted on next steps.

how_do_i_submit_my_bid_or_estimate.answer: Vendors can submit bids by replying to the email with their quote and availability, or by clicking the 'Enter Bid' button in the job email to submit directly.
talking_points: Vendors can reply via email with bid amount and availability
Use 'Enter Bid' button to submit directly to owner for approval
Include visit fee, total cost, and earliest availability in bid
how_do_i_submit_my_bid_or_estimate.suggested_response: We sent you an email requesting your bid for this job. May we please know your bid or visit fee and your soonest availability? You may submit your bid using the 'Enter Bid' button provided in our previous email or reply through email. Thank you.

how_do_i_upload_invoice_and_photos.answer: Vendors should reply to the job email with invoice and completion photos attached, or use the 'Upload Invoice & Photos' button provided in job update emails.
talking_points: Reply to job email with invoice and photos as attachments
Use 'Upload Invoice & Photos' button if unable to upload through system
Confirm all required documentation is included before closing job
how_do_i_upload_invoice_and_photos.suggested_response: Thank you for letting us know. Please reply back to this email with the invoice and pictures, so we can process and update the ticket on our end.

what_payment_methods_do_you_accept.answer: Ziprent only accepts ACH direct deposit. Vendors must provide bank account details (account number, routing number, bank name, account name) to receive payment.
talking_points: ACH direct deposit is the only payment method available
No checks, credit cards, Zelle, PayPal, Venmo, or CashApp accepted
Require account number, routing number, bank name, and name on account
what_payment_methods_do_you_accept.suggested_response: We make payments via direct deposit only, which is our preferred method. Please provide the following to receive payment within three business days to your bank account: Account number, Routing number, Bank's Name, and Name on the Account.

what_is_the_job_number_for_this_invoice.answer: When invoices are received without job reference, Ziprent staff need vendors to provide the job number or property address to match and process the payment.
talking_points: Request job number or work order number from vendor
Ask for property address if job number is unknown
Confirm details match system records before processing
what_is_the_job_number_for_this_invoice.suggested_response: Hello, what is the job # for this invoice? Or can you provide the property address so we can locate it in our system? Thanks.

invoice_is_unclear_or_unreadable.answer: When invoices are unreadable, cut off, or improperly formatted, Ziprent requests vendors to resend in a clear, readable format (typically PDF).
talking_points: Request invoice be resent as clear PDF attachment
Specify what information is missing or unreadable
Cannot process payment until legible invoice is received
invoice_is_unclear_or_unreadable.suggested_response: Hello, when I open this invoice it cuts off the sides and I cannot see anything. Can you please resend as a clear PDF? Thanks.

can_you_confirm_payment_was_made.answer: Ziprent provides payment confirmation including payment date, reimbursement date, vendor name, payment method (ACH with last 4 digits), and amount paid.
talking_points: Provide specific payment date and reimbursement date
Include payment method (ACH) and last 4 digits of account
Confirm exact amount paid and job category
can_you_confirm_payment_was_made.suggested_response: This was already paid. Paid: [date], Reimbursed: [date], Payment: ACH [Vendor Name] (x[last 4 digits]), $[amount] Paid.

how_do_i_schedule_appointment_with_tenan.answer: When a job is approved, vendors are instructed to contact the tenant directly using provided contact information to schedule an appointment, then confirm the date/time with Ziprent.
talking_points: Use 'Confirm Appointment' button after scheduling with tenant
Contact tenant directly using phone/email provided in job details
Notify Ziprent of confirmed date and time window
how_do_i_schedule_appointment_with_tenan.suggested_response: Vendor Action Required: Call the tenant below to schedule an appointment. Use 'Confirm Appointment' to notify us of the date and time once an appointment is confirmed with the tenant.

owner_wants_to_use_their_own_vendor.answer: Property owners can choose to use their own vendor instead of Ziprent's network. Ziprent will update the job accordingly and exclude network vendors from the ticket.
talking_points: Update job status to 'External Vendor (Owner Vendor)'
Stop automated emails to network vendors
Coordinate directly with owner's chosen vendor
owner_wants_to_use_their_own_vendor.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. Per our conversation, you informed me that you had already sent your handyman to take care of this repair. We will update this ticket to an external vendor so that no more automated approval emails will be sent.

invoice_amount_doesnt_match_approved_bid.answer: When invoice amounts don't match the approved bid, Ziprent requests clarification from the vendor and may ask for an updated invoice reflecting the correct amount.
talking_points: Reference the originally approved bid amount
Request itemized explanation for any discrepancies
Ask for corrected invoice if charges are incorrect
invoice_amount_doesnt_match_approved_bid.suggested_response: Please send us an updated invoice reflecting the correct amount. The visit fee approved for the job order is $[amount] only.

vendor_not_interested_or_unavailable.answer: When vendors decline jobs or are unavailable, Ziprent thanks them, excludes them from that job, and reassigns to another vendor.
talking_points: Thank vendor for prompt response
Confirm exclusion from current job updates
Express interest in future opportunities
vendor_not_interested_or_unavailable.suggested_response: Thank you for letting us know, we will reassign this job to another vendor and see you on the next one!

what_is_status_of_pending_invoice.answer: When invoices are pending, Ziprent may be waiting for owner approval, tenant confirmation, or additional documentation before processing payment.
talking_points: Explain specific reason for delay (owner approval, verification, etc.)
Provide expected timeline when available
Request any missing information needed to proceed
what_is_status_of_pending_invoice.suggested_response: The invoices for [address] are still pending as we wait on confirmation from [owner/tenant]. We will process payment as soon as we receive approval.

need_to_follow_up_on_job_completion.answer: Ziprent follows up with vendors after scheduled appointments to confirm job completion and request invoices/photos if work was successfully completed.
talking_points: Ask if issue was resolved during visit
Request upload of invoice and completion photos
Provide buttons/links for invoice submission if resolved
need_to_follow_up_on_job_completion.suggested_response: How did the visit go, and could you resolve the issue? If the issue is resolved, upload photos/invoices using the button below. Otherwise, please update the job notes to keep everyone informed.

owner_asking_about_repair_charges.answer: Property owners may inquire about repair costs, request clarification on invoices, or question whether they should pay for certain repairs.
talking_points: Explain what work was completed and why
Clarify owner vs. tenant responsibility based on cause
Confirm if owner wants Ziprent to pay on their behalf or handle directly
owner_asking_about_repair_charges.suggested_response: We have sent your invoice to the owner to inquire if they will be paying this directly to you. We will update you once we hear back.

missing_attachments_or_photos.answer: When vendors mention sending attachments but they are not received, Ziprent notifies them and requests resubmission.
talking_points: Confirm no attachments were received
Request vendor resend via email reply
Verify file format and size are acceptable
missing_attachments_or_photos.suggested_response: Hello, there were no pictures attached. Can you please resend them? Thanks.

recap_phone_conversation.answer: After phone calls with vendors, tenants, or owners, Ziprent staff send email recaps documenting what was discussed and agreed upon.
talking_points: Summarize key points discussed during call
Confirm any commitments or next steps
Create written record for documentation
recap_phone_conversation.suggested_response: Thank you for speaking with me. Just to recap our call: [summary of discussion]. Please let us know if we have anything incorrect or if you have additional questions.

need_tenant_contact_information.answer: Vendors need tenant contact details to coordinate property access and schedule appointments. Ziprent provides this in job assignment emails.
talking_points: Tenant contact info included in job approval/assignment emails
Provide name, phone number, and email
Confirm scheduling instructions and access details
need_tenant_contact_information.suggested_response: Please find the tenant contact information below: [Name], [Phone], [Email]. Please coordinate with them to schedule your visit and use the 'Confirm Appointment' button to notify us of the scheduled date and time.

work_not_approved_or_no_job_order.answer: When vendors submit invoices for work that wasn't pre-approved or lacks a job order, Ziprent requests clarification and reminds that work must be approved before dispatch.
talking_points: Confirm whether work was tenant-initiated without approval
Explain that all repairs must be pre-approved by owner through Ziprent
Request job order number or details to verify authorization
work_not_approved_or_no_job_order.suggested_response: Can you please clarify this invoice? There is no job order opened at this address. Please be advised that all job orders, especially sending out vendors, and all repair costs, should be approved by the owner first.

warranty_repair_request.answer: For issues covered under warranty from previous work, Ziprent coordinates with the original vendor or builder to initiate warranty service at no cost to owner.
talking_points: Reference original job number and warranty terms
Contact builder/vendor about warranty coverage
Emphasize urgency if issue impacts habitability
warranty_repair_request.suggested_response: We hope this message finds you well. The tenant has initiated a repair request pertaining to the same issue that occurred earlier, related to [issue]. Please let us know if you may initiate a warranty request so we may move forward and schedule a visit at the earliest.

invoice_received_acknowledgment.answer: When vendors submit invoices, Ziprent acknowledges receipt and confirms they will review and process shortly or inform the owner.
talking_points: Acknowledge receipt of invoice
Confirm next steps (review, owner notification, processing)
Provide timeline if available
invoice_received_acknowledgment.suggested_response: Hello, we got the invoice! We will review and process shortly. Thanks.

need_bid_before_owner_approval.answer: Owners may request to see bids/quotes before deciding whether to approve a repair. Ziprent collects estimates from vendors for owner review.
talking_points: Gather bids from multiple vendors when requested
Send bids to owner for review and approval
Await owner decision before scheduling work
need_bid_before_owner_approval.suggested_response: No worries, we'll gather bids for this job and send them to you for review before proceeding.

job_marked_complete_confirmation.answer: When vendors confirm job completion, Ziprent thanks them, marks the job complete, and closes the ticket in their system.
talking_points: Thank vendor for update and completion
Confirm job will be marked complete
Close ticket to stop further automated emails
job_marked_complete_confirmation.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out and letting us know that this job has been completed. We will go ahead and mark this as complete and close the ticket.

owner_will_handle_payment_directly.answer: When owners pay vendors directly rather than through Ziprent, Ziprent confirms this arrangement and notes it in the system to avoid duplicate payment.
talking_points: Confirm owner is handling payment directly with vendor
Note in system to prevent processing invoice
Close ticket as complete with external payment
owner_will_handle_payment_directly.suggested_response: Hello, will you be paying this directly? Please confirm so we can update our records accordingly.

what_is_ach_transaction_fee.answer: Ziprent charges a 5% transaction fee on ACH payments, which is debited from the vendor's bank account at the time payment is received.
talking_points: 5% processing fee applies to all ACH transactions
Fee is automatically deducted when payment is deposited
This is standard for the payment processing service
what_is_ach_transaction_fee.suggested_response: Please note that it is a 5% transaction fee which is debited at the time of payment from the bank account receiving ACH payment for the completed work. Have a great day!

need_vendor_email_for_coordination.answer: When coordinating with owner's vendors, Ziprent requests email addresses to add vendors to job communications and send invoicing instructions.
talking_points: Request vendor email address for job updates
Needed to coordinate scheduling and invoice submission
Helps maintain communication trail
need_vendor_email_for_coordination.suggested_response: Thank you for the information. Do you have their email address as well so we can coordinate directly?

clarify_work_performed.answer: When invoices are unclear about what work was done, Ziprent asks vendors to clarify the specific services performed and match them to the original work order.
talking_points: Reference original work order description
Ask vendor to explain discrepancies
Request itemized details of work completed
clarify_work_performed.suggested_response: Can you check this invoice? Job [number] is for [original request]. Please clarify what work was performed.

following_up_with_owner_for_approval.answer: When awaiting owner decisions on bids or repairs, Ziprent follows up to ensure timely approval and keep vendors/tenants informed of status.
talking_points: Actively following up with owner
Keep vendor/tenant updated on approval status
Emphasize time sensitivity for urgent repairs
following_up_with_owner_for_approval.suggested_response: We're actively following up with the owner regarding this matter, and have not received a response yet. We will keep you posted as soon as we hear back.

exclude_vendor_from_job_type.answer: When vendors only perform specific services, Ziprent excludes them from irrelevant job types to avoid unnecessary emails.
talking_points: Respect vendor specialization and preferences
Update vendor profile to exclude certain job categories
Apologize for any inconvenience
exclude_vendor_from_job_type.suggested_response: Thank you for getting back to us and for letting us know. We will exclude you from this job type going forward.

invoice_has_late_fees_or_past_due.answer: When vendors report overdue invoices or late fees, Ziprent investigates payment status, addresses delays, and may dispute late fees if there are outstanding questions.
talking_points: Check internal records for payment status
Identify reason for delay (missing info, owner questions, etc.)
Address late fee concerns if delay wasn't vendor's fault
invoice_has_late_fees_or_past_due.suggested_response: This has not been paid as there are outstanding questions from the property owner. Please review the email we just sent and get back to us at your earliest convenience. Until then, no late fees should be accrued as we have outstanding questions that no one has responded to.

tenant_responsibility_vs_owner.answer: Ziprent helps determine whether repair costs should be covered by owner or charged to tenant based on cause of damage and vendor findings.
talking_points: Request vendor report on cause of issue
Review lease terms for maintenance responsibilities
Owner makes final decision on who pays
tenant_responsibility_vs_owner.suggested_response: We would like to ask if this work order is something the tenant communicated directly before they hired a vendor. In line with charging the tenants, the vendor should have a report stating that the tenants caused the issue.

reschedule_appointment_due_to_tenant.answer: When tenants need to reschedule due to illness, conflicts, or other reasons, Ziprent coordinates between vendor and tenant for a new appointment time.
talking_points: Acknowledge rescheduling need
Request vendor update system when new time is confirmed
Maintain communication between all parties
reschedule_appointment_due_to_tenant.suggested_response: Thanks for the update. Please let us know once the tenants reach back out and the date and time are set so we can update our system accordingly.

owner_wants_to_cancel_job.answer: Property owners can cancel job orders if they decide to handle repairs differently or if circumstances change. Ziprent updates the system to stop vendor outreach.
talking_points: Confirm cancellation with owner
Stop all vendor bidding and scheduling
Update job status and close ticket
owner_wants_to_cancel_job.suggested_response: Please cancel this bid. [Reason provided by owner]. We will update the system accordingly.

parts_covered_under_warranty.answer: For warranty repairs, Ziprent clarifies what should be covered (parts vs. labor) and coordinates with vendors to ensure proper billing.
talking_points: Reference warranty terms (90-day parts, 30-day labor, etc.)
Question charges that should be covered under warranty
Request itemized invoice showing what's charged
parts_covered_under_warranty.suggested_response: We should not have needed to pay for parts/materials, as we are still under our 90-day warranty. Please check back with the company to find out why these additional charges were included on the invoice.

micro_deposits_for_ach_verification.answer: When vendors add bank accounts for ACH payment, Ziprent sends micro-deposits (two small amounts) to verify the account before processing payments.
talking_points: Two small deposits will appear within 1-2 business days
Used to verify bank account for security
Regular payments will follow after verification
micro_deposits_for_ach_verification.suggested_response: The micro-deposits should be there by today. It will be 2 micro deposits. Once verified, we can process your payment.

confirm_work_order_details_before_biddin.answer: Before collecting bids, Ziprent confirms work order details with owners to ensure vendors receive accurate information for quoting.
talking_points: Review work order description with owner
Ask if any additional details should be added
Get confirmation before sending to vendors
confirm_work_order_details_before_biddin.suggested_response: Please check the work order and let us know if you would like to add anything additional before we start collecting bids.

remind_tenants_not_to_self_repair.answer: When tenants attempt repairs themselves, Ziprent reminds them this can cause damage and violates lease terms, and that they must report issues promptly instead.
talking_points: Reference lease clause about alterations/repairs
Explain risks of tenant self-repair
Instruct to report issues immediately to management
remind_tenants_not_to_self_repair.suggested_response: We'll remind the tenants not to make any repairs or alterations on their own, as this can cause damage to the property, and that they should promptly notify us of any malfunctions as stated in their lease.

how_to_submit_maintenance_request.answer: Tenants must submit all maintenance requests online through their Ziprent account portal under the Repairs tab.
talking_points: Login at www.ziprent.com/auth/login
Navigate to the Repairs tab in left side panel
Click 'Start Repair' and enter information
Use 'forgot password' feature if needed
how_to_submit_maintenance_request.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. All maintenance requests must be submitted online. Please login to your Ziprent account at www.ziprent.com/auth/login and navigate to the Repairs tab in the left side panel. If you have forgotten your password, click forgot password and follow the steps. For further assistance, please visit our FAQ which includes instructions on how to submit a maintenance request.

how_to_confirm_vendor_scheduling.answer: Vendors should email Ziprent once they have scheduled a day and time with the tenant for approved jobs.
talking_points: Email confirmation required after scheduling with tenant
Include scheduled date and time
Ziprent will add visit to calendar
Respond to original job email thread
how_to_confirm_vendor_scheduling.suggested_response: Great, we'll add the visit to our calendar. Please send us an email once a day and time has been scheduled between you and the tenant(s).

how_to_add_bank_account_for_payments.answer: Owners/vendors can add bank account information through the Ziprent portal by logging in and entering banking details with security verification.
talking_points: Login to Ziprent portal
Navigate to payment/banking section
Enter bank login credentials
Verify with security code sent to phone
Confirm setup was successful
how_to_add_bank_account_for_payments.suggested_response: Please let us know if this did not work for you. If you need any further assistance feel free to reach out to us.

how_to_request_garage_opener_replacement.answer: For garage opener/clicker replacements, Ziprent will create a repair ticket and contact the building manager first before engaging vendors.
talking_points: Repair thread initiated to track progress
Building manager contacted first for replacement
Security incidents (theft/break-in) reported to building
Tenant cc'd on communications
Alternative vendors engaged if needed
how_to_request_garage_opener_replacement.suggested_response: Sorry to hear that happened to you. We will initiate a repair thread to have this incident noted and track the progress in our system. Since this is a request for a garage clicker, before we connect with one of our vendors we'll reach out to the building manager to see if they can provide you with a replacement. It will be good to notify them of the break in too. We'll cc you on the email to the building manager.

what_to_do_with_previous_tenant_mail.answer: Mail belonging to previous tenants or homeowners should be returned to sender; current tenants should notify Ziprent who will follow up with property owners.
talking_points: Confirm mail does not belong to current tenant
Ziprent follows up with homeowner
Mail typically belongs to former tenant or owner
Thank tenant for reporting and assistance
what_to_do_with_previous_tenant_mail.suggested_response: Thank you so much for reaching out and providing us with that information. We will follow up with the homeowner further, and we greatly appreciate your assistance.

how_to_respond_to_missed_calls.answer: When calls are missed, Ziprent sends follow-up emails apologizing and asking if assistance is still needed, directing to call back or email on existing thread.
talking_points: Acknowledge missed call and apologize
Ask if assistance still needed
Provide callback number
Direct to email on existing repair thread
Offer context if known (e.g., bids sent for approval)
how_to_respond_to_missed_calls.suggested_response: Hello, Ziprent Repairs here. We apologize for missing your call. Please let us know if you still need any assistance. You may call back or email us on the email thread opened for your concern. Thank you.

what_to_do_about_lockbox_with_no_key.answer: When vendors report empty lockboxes, Ziprent acknowledges the issue and thanks them for the update.
talking_points: Acknowledge empty lockbox report
Thank vendor for notification
No further action required from vendor
Issue documented internally
what_to_do_about_lockbox_with_no_key.suggested_response: Thank you for the update. Have a great night!

how_to_decline_vendor_quote_when_another.answer: When a vendor has already been assigned, Ziprent politely declines other quotes and promises to contact them for future opportunities.
talking_points: Thank vendor for quote submission
Inform that another vendor already assigned
Keep vendor on file for future jobs
Maintain positive relationship
how_to_decline_vendor_quote_when_another.suggested_response: Thank you so much for sharing the quote for this job. Unfortunately, a vendor has already been assigned to this ticket. We will be sure to let you know about future job opportunities. Thank you again and have a wonderful day!

how_to_handle_forwarded_vendor_updates.answer: When tenants forward vendor scheduling updates, Ziprent verifies if there's an open ticket and offers to create one if needed.
talking_points: Thank tenant for forwarding information
Check for existing open tickets
Verify if tenant requested the service
Offer to open ticket if issue exists
Clarify confusion if no request was made
how_to_handle_forwarded_vendor_updates.suggested_response: Thank you for forwarding this email to us. We do not see any open tickets for a HVAC at your property. Please let us know if there is an issue and you would like us to open a ticket.

how_to_handle_manufacturer_alerts_forwar.answer: When tenants forward manufacturer alerts (like battery replacement warnings), Ziprent contacts the property owner and may request additional information from tenant.
talking_points: Acknowledge the manufacturer alert
Contact property owner about issue
Request specific details from tenant (e.g., beeping sounds)
Relay information between tenant and owner
Track for resolution
how_to_handle_manufacturer_alerts_forwar.suggested_response: Regarding the garage door backup battery: The property owner has reached out to the manufacturer, and they would like to know if you hear any beeping sounds when you press the button to open the garage door. Please let us know so that we can notify the property owner accordingly.

how_to_clarify_incomplete_vendor_message.answer: When vendors send unclear messages or incomplete job numbers, Ziprent asks for clarification on which job/property is being referenced.
talking_points: Politely request clarification
Ask for job number or property address
Maintain professional tone
Cannot proceed without proper identification
how_to_clarify_incomplete_vendor_message.suggested_response: Can you kindly confirm what job/property this is in relation to?

what_to_do_when_repair_completed_but_no_.answer: When a tenant reports a completed repair that wasn't tracked, Ziprent apologizes for delay and requests photos to initiate a job order retroactively.
talking_points: Apologize for delayed response or lack of tracking
Request photos of issue/repair
Initiate job order to document work
Ensure proper record keeping
Thank tenant for notification
what_to_do_when_repair_completed_but_no_.suggested_response: We apologize for the delay in response. We will initiate a job order. Can you send us a photo related to the leak?

how_to_handle_proof_of_income_questions.answer: For tenants with international businesses, proof of income is required even if the individual is based locally.
talking_points: Proof of income required regardless of business location
Individual must be based locally
Business registration location flexible
Standard application requirements apply
how_to_handle_proof_of_income_questions.suggested_response: That would work; he would need to provide proof of income. Feel free to reach out if you have any questions or concerns; we will gladly assist you.

how_to_respond_when_vendor_declines_job.answer: When property owners choose other vendors after bids received, Ziprent thanks the declined vendor and offers to contact them for future opportunities.
talking_points: Thank vendor for prompt response
Inform that owner selected different vendor
Keep vendor in system for future work
Maintain positive relationship
Professional closure
how_to_respond_when_vendor_declines_job.suggested_response: Thank you for your prompt response. The property owner has decided to proceed with another vendor for this work order. We will reach out to you should we require your assistance.

how_to_handle_onsite_price_quotes_from_v.answer: When vendors provide pricing while onsite, they should confirm tenant name, address, and price via email before proceeding with work.
talking_points: Include tenant name and full address
State total job cost clearly
Request approval before proceeding
Email confirmation required
Wait for Ziprent approval if needed
how_to_handle_onsite_price_quotes_from_v.suggested_response: We need confirmation of the details. Please provide: tenant name, full property address, description of issue found, total cost, and whether you're waiting for approval or already proceeding.

how_to_handle_electrical_panel_location_.answer: When tenants cannot locate breaker panels, owners should provide specific location details and instruct on resetting breakers before sending vendors.
talking_points: Provide specific panel location (e.g., left side of front yard)
Instruct tenant to reset breaker first
May be overloaded circuit issue
Follow up to confirm if issue resolved
Send vendor only if problem persists
how_to_handle_electrical_panel_location_.suggested_response: Thank you for the email. We will inform the tenants and get back to you.

how_to_close_completed_repair_tickets.answer: When vendors or tenants confirm repairs are complete, Ziprent thanks them and closes the work order.
talking_points: Thank vendor/tenant for update
Confirm work completion
Close work order in system
Brief, professional acknowledgment
how_to_close_completed_repair_tickets.suggested_response: Thank you for the update. We will close this work order shortly.

how_to_resend_bid_request_emails.answer: When vendors receive texts but not emails with job details, Ziprent resends the bid request email and confirms the recipient address.
talking_points: Acknowledge missing email issue
Resend bid request to vendor email
Provide email subject line for reference
Confirm correct email address on file
Offer further assistance
how_to_resend_bid_request_emails.suggested_response: Thank you for your prompt response! I have gone ahead and sent the bid request email out again. Kindly note that the message is subjected as follows: [subject line]. The message has been routed to [email]. Should you have any other questions or concerns, please feel free to reach out!

how_to_handle_lease_renewal_rent_increas.answer: When owners propose rent increases, Ziprent confirms the amount and asks whether renewal is for one year or month-to-month.
talking_points: Acknowledge proposed increase amount
Confirm lease term preference (annual vs monthly)
Standard renewal process follows
Document owner's decision
Prepare renewal documents accordingly
how_to_handle_lease_renewal_rent_increas.suggested_response: Understood. Are you looking to renew for a year or go to a monthly term?

what_pests_are_tenant_responsibility.answer: Per lease agreements, common household pests like ants, cockroaches, spiders, small bee nests, and rodents are the tenant's responsibility to address.
talking_points: Reference signed lease agreement
List covered pests: ants, cockroaches, spiders, small bees, rodents
Tenant may hire their own pest control vendor
Owner not responsible for common household pests
Different rules may apply for infestations or structural issues
what_pests_are_tenant_responsibility.suggested_response: Please be advised that per the signed lease agreement, other common household pests, including ants, cockroaches, spiders, small bee nests, and rodents are the Tenant's responsibility. You may hire your pest control vendor regarding this.

how_to_handle_water_leak_alerts_from_uti.answer: When water companies notify owners of potential leaks, Ziprent contacts tenants immediately to confirm if they've noticed running faucets or toilets requiring plumber attention.
talking_points: Urgent response required for leak alerts
Check for running faucets or toilets
May affect multiple units if shared water
Plumber scheduled if confirmed
Follow up on repairs and water usage
how_to_handle_water_leak_alerts_from_uti.suggested_response: I hope this finds you well. The property owner had reached out to inform that the water company had notified them of a potential leak at the property, likely due to a running faucet or toilet over the last 24 hours or so. They wanted to confirm if you had noticed anything like this so we can have a plumber sent out to address this promptly.

how_to_submit_repair_instead_of_direct_e.answer: When tenants email directly about repairs instead of using the portal, Ziprent provides instructions to submit through the proper repair ticket system.
talking_points: All repairs must go through portal
Login at www.ziprent.com/auth/login
Navigate to Repairs tab
Click 'Start Repair' button
Password reset available if needed
how_to_submit_repair_instead_of_direct_e.suggested_response: Thank you for letting us know about this. Please proceed with submitting a repair ticket through the portal so the issue can be addressed accordingly. You can request a repair by following the instructions below: Please login to your Ziprent account at www.ziprent.com/auth/login, navigate to the Repairs tab in the left side panel, then click 'Start Repair' and enter the information in the fields.

how_to_handle_pet_blocking_landscaping_a.answer: When pets prevent landscapers from accessing yards, Ziprent notifies tenants of the schedule and reminds them to secure pets during service times.
talking_points: Notify tenant of landscaping schedule
Remind to secure/remove pets from service area
Incomplete work due to safety concerns
Reschedule or coordinate for completion
May identify other issues (gopher activity, etc.)
how_to_handle_pet_blocking_landscaping_a.suggested_response: Thank you for reaching out. We've notified the tenant of the landscaping schedule and reminded her to keep her dog out of the backyard during service times so the landscapers can complete their work. Should you require any further assistance, please feel free to reach out.

how_to_handle_vendor_key_return.answer: When vendors borrow keys, Ziprent coordinates return location (typically lockbox) and confirms with owner for pickup.
talking_points: Confirm key return location with owner
Typically returned to property lockbox
Coordinate timing for owner pickup
Vendor may not be registered in system
Access codes shared as needed
how_to_handle_vendor_key_return.suggested_response: Thank you for confirming that. We had to share the access codes with you since we did not have this vendor as an associated vendor to this property. We'd appreciate it if you could share it with him. Let us know if anything else is required.

how_to_escalate_unanswered_repair_questi.answer: When owners have unanswered questions about repairs, Ziprent acknowledges receipt and notifies the Repairs team for review and response.
talking_points: Acknowledge that questions were received
Notify appropriate team for review
Ensure timely response to follow
May relate to approval requests or prior work
Escalation to proper department
how_to_escalate_unanswered_repair_questi.suggested_response: We have notified the Repairs team that you have sent some questions for their review.

what_services_does_ziprent_offer.answer: Ziprent offers property management ($150/month for first unit, $100 for additional units) and tenant placement ($1,250-$1,500 one-time fee). Services can be selected separately or combined.
talking_points: Property Management: $150/month flat fee for first unit, $100/month for additional units
Tenant Placement: $1,250-$1,500 one-time fee (varies by market)
Services are unbundled - select one or both based on your needs
Photography fee: $250 charged when property is listed
Month-to-month contract with no commitments or penalties to cancel
what_services_does_ziprent_offer.suggested_response: Thanks for reaching out! We offer two main services: Property Management at $150/month flat fee (no percentages or hidden costs), and Tenant Placement for a one-time fee of $1,250-$1,500. You can choose either service independently or combine them based on your needs. Our contract is month-to-month with no commitments, and you wouldn't owe anything until we perform. Would you like to schedule a call to discuss your specific property needs?

can_i_get_tenant_placement_only_without_.answer: Yes, tenant placement can be purchased separately without property management. After signing the PMA, owners select which service(s) they want and are only billed for what they choose.
talking_points: Tenant placement is available as a standalone service
The Property Management Agreement (PMA) is a general agreement covering all services
After signing, you select which specific service(s) you want
You are only billed for the services you actually select
Can easily add property management later with the click of a button
can_i_get_tenant_placement_only_without_.suggested_response: Absolutely! The Property Management Agreement is a general agreement that covers all the services you're eligible for, but signing it doesn't mean you're signing up for everything. Once signed, the system will ask you to select tenant placement, property management, or both. You'll only be billed for the service(s) you select. If you ever want to add property management later, you can do so with just a click of a button.

how_do_i_get_a_market_analysis_for_my_pr.answer: A market analysis is provided after initial contact. It shows historical averages for comparable properties in the area but doesn't reflect current active listings.
talking_points: Market analysis is provided complimentary during the consultation process
Shows historical rent averages for comparable properties in your area
Based on bed/bath count and location data
Does not show how current active listings are performing

Do we pay a compensation for the buyers.answer: No, Ziprent does not pay compensation or commissions to agents who bring in prospective tenants.
talking_points: No, Ziprent does not pay compensation or commissions to agents who bring in prospective tenants.
• No Commissions: Ziprent does not pay out agent commissions for bringing in prospective tenants.

• No MLS Listings: Ziprent does not advertise on the MLS (Multiple Listing Service). Listing on the MLS would require paying a cooperating agent commission, which would make Ziprent's flat-rate fee structure impossible.
• Referral Program: While commissions are not paid for tenants, agents (or anyone else) can participate in Ziprent's referral program if they refer homeowners (new clients) to the company.
